<p id="mwDQ">Presidential elections in the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philippines" id="mwDg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Philippines">Philippines</a> were held on May 9, 2022, as part of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2022_Philippine_general_election" id="mwDw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2022 Philippine general election">2022 general election</a>. This was the 17th direct presidential election and 15th vice presidential election in the country since <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1935_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwEA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1935 Philippine presidential election">1935</a>, and the sixth <a class="extiw" href="https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/sextennial" id="mwEQ" rel="mw:WikiLink/Interwiki" title="wikt:sextennial">sextennial</a> presidential and vice presidential election since <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1992_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwEg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1992 Philippine presidential election">1992</a>.</p>
<p id="mwEw">Incumbent president <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rodrigo_Duterte" id="mwFA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rodrigo Duterte">Rodrigo Duterte</a> was ineligible for re-election because the president is limited to a single term under the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Constitution_of_the_Philippines" id="mwFQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Constitution of the Philippines">1987 Philippine Constitution</a>. Incumbent vice president <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leni_Robredo" id="mwFg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leni Robredo">Leni Robredo</a> was eligible for re-election but chose to run for the presidency instead. Therefore, this election determined the 17th <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_of_the_Philippines" id="mwFw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="President of the Philippines">president</a> and the 15th <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vice_President_of_the_Philippines" id="mwGA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Vice President of the Philippines">vice president</a>. The president and vice president are elected separately, so the two winning candidates can come from different political parties.</p>
<p id="mwGQ">The election took place amidst the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic_in_the_Philippines" id="mwGg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="COVID-19 pandemic in the Philippines">COVID-19 pandemic</a> which had caused the country's economy to fall into <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COVID-19_recession" id="mwGw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="COVID-19 recession">recession</a>.<sup about="#mwt38" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"covidrecession"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-covidrecession-2"}}' id="cite_ref-covidrecession_2-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-covidrecession-2" id="mwHA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwHQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwHg">[</span>1<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwHw">]</span></span></a></sup> Other key issues were the continuation of President <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Presidency_of_Rodrigo_Duterte" id="mwIA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Presidency of Rodrigo Duterte">Duterte's policies</a>, a re-examination of the country's foreign relationships in response to its <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Territorial_disputes_in_the_South_China_Sea" id="mwIQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Territorial disputes in the South China Sea">territorial dispute</a> with <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/China" id="mwIg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="China">China</a>, management of the country's debt, rising inflation, and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Climate_change" id="mwIw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Climate change">climate change</a>.<sup about="#mwt41" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":29"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:29-3"}}' id="cite_ref-:29_3-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:29-3" id="mwJA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwJQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwJg">[</span>2<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwJw">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt44" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":30"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:30-4"}}' id="cite_ref-:30_4-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:30-4" id="mwKA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwKQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwKg">[</span>3<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwKw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwLA">The ticket of former senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwLQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mayor_of_Davao_City" id="mwLg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mayor of Davao City">Davao City mayor</a> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sara_Duterte" id="mwLw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sara Duterte">Sara Duterte</a> won the presidency and vice presidency respectively, defeating incumbent vice president Leni Robredo and incumbent senator <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Francis_Pangilinan" id="mwMA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Francis Pangilinan">Francis Pangilinan</a> in a landslide. It was the first election since the establishment of the Fifth Republic in 1987 where the president and vice president were elected by a majority, and the first election since <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2004_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwMQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2004 Philippine presidential election">2004</a> where the winning president and vice president came from the same presidential ticket.<sup about="#mwt45" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":31"}}' id="cite_ref-:31_5-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:31-5" id="mwMg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwMw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwNA">[</span>4<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwNQ">]</span></span></a></sup> The election saw the highest turnout since <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1998_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwNg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1998 Philippine presidential election">1998</a>, with about 56 million voters participating. Marcos received 31.6 million votes, the most votes ever cast for a candidate in a presidential election in the Philippines, while Duterte received 32.2 million votes, the most votes ever cast for a candidate in the country.</p>
<p id="mwNw">Marcos became the second president from <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ilocos_Norte" id="mwOA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ilocos Norte">Ilocos Norte</a> after his father, former president 
<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ferdinand_Marcos" id="mwOQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ferdinand Marcos">Ferdinand Marcos</a>,<sup about="#mwt46" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"proclamation-may25"}}' id="cite_ref-proclamation-may25_6-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-proclamation-may25-6" id="mwOg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwOw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwPA">[</span>5<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwPQ">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt49" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-7"}}' id="cite_ref-7" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-7" id="mwPg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwPw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwQA">[</span>6<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwQQ">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt52" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-8"}}' id="cite_ref-8" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-8" id="mwQg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwQw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwRA">[</span>7<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwRQ">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt55" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-9"}}' id="cite_ref-9" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-9" id="mwRg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwRw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwSA">[</span>8<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwSQ">]</span></span></a></sup> while Duterte became the first vice president from <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_City" id="mwSg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao City">Davao City</a>, the third vice president to come from <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mindanao" id="mwSw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mindanao">Mindanao</a> after <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Emmanuel_Pelaez" id="mwTA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Emmanuel Pelaez">Emmanuel Pelaez</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teofisto_Guingona_Jr." id="mwTQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Teofisto Guingona Jr.">Teofisto Guingona Jr.</a>, and the youngest to be elected.<sup about="#mwt58" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-10"}}' id="cite_ref-10" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-10" id="mwTg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwTw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwUA">[</span>9<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwUQ">]</span></span></a></sup> This also marked the return of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marcos_family" id="mwUg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Marcos family">Marcos family</a> to power for the first time since the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/People_Power_Revolution" id="mwUw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="People Power Revolution">People Power Revolution</a>.<sup about="#mwt61" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Associated Press"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Associated_Press-11"}}' id="cite_ref-Associated_Press_11-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Associated_Press-11" id="mwVA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwVQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwVg">[</span>10<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwVw">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt64" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-12"}}' id="cite_ref-12" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-12" id="mwWA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwWQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwWg">[</span>11<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwWw">]</span></span></a></sup><!-- This reference is already used in this article. v<ref name="WaPo"&#x3E;{{cite news |last1=Cabato |first1=Regine |last2=Westfall |first2=Sammy |title=Marcos family once ousted by uprising wins Philippines vote in landslide |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2022/05/10/philippines&#x2D;presidential&#x2D;election&#x2D;result&#x2D;ferdinand&#x2D;bongbong&#x2D;marcos/ |access&#x2D;date=May 12, 2022 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |date=May 10, 2022 |archive&#x2D;date=May 10, 2022 If you find a reliable source that specifically mentions this, AND NOT THE WAPO article, as again, even the term "far&#x2D;right" is not stated on that article, use that as a reference. This election also helped bolster the [[far&#x2D;right politics|far&#x2D;right]] in the Philippines.<ref name="WaPo"&#x3E;{{cite news |last1=Cabato |first1=Regine |last2=Westfall |first2=Sammy |title=Marcos family once ousted by uprising wins Philippines vote in landslide |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2022/05/10/philippines&#x2D;presidential&#x2D;election&#x2D;result&#x2D;ferdinand&#x2D;bongbong&#x2D;marcos/ |access&#x2D;date=May 12, 2022 |newspaper=[[The Washington Post]] |date=May 10, 2022 |archive&#x2D;date=May 10, 2022 |archive&#x2D;url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220510073822/https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2022/05/10/philippines&#x2D;presidential&#x2D;election&#x2D;result&#x2D;ferdinand&#x2D;bongbong&#x2D;marcos/ |url&#x2D;status=live }}</ref&#x3E;--> Marcos was <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inauguration_of_Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwXA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Inauguration of Bongbong Marcos">inaugurated</a> on June 30, 2022, while Duterte was inaugurated earlier on June 19, 2022. The election and subsequent transfer of power marked another large-scale democratic transition conducted through the Philippines' national electoral system.</p>
<meta data-mw='{"autoGenerated":true}' id="mwXQ" property="mw:PageProp/toc"/></section><section aria-labelledby="Background" data-mw-section-id="1" id="mwXg"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Background">Background</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=1" title="Edit section: Background"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt65" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"See also","href":"./Template:See_also"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"2016 Philippine presidential election"},"2":{"wt":"2019 Philippine Senate election"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwXw" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><style about="#mwt66" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' data-mw-deduplicate="TemplateStyles:r1353705441" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles">.mw-parser-output .hatnote{font-style:italic}.mw-parser-output div.hatnote{padding-left:1.6em;margin-bottom:0.5em}.mw-parser-output .hatnote i{font-style:normal}.mw-parser-output .hatnote+span.mw-empty-elt+.hatnote,.mw-parser-output .hatnote+link+.hatnote{margin-top:-0.5em}@media print{body.ns-0 .mw-parser-output .hatnote{display:none!important}}</style></span><div about="#mwt65" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwYA" role="note">See also: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2016_Philippine_presidential_election" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2016 Philippine presidential election">2016 Philippine presidential election</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2019_Philippine_Senate_election" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2019 Philippine Senate election">2019 Philippine Senate election</a></div>
<figure class="mw-default-size" id="mwYQ" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Ph15-071316.jpg" id="mwYg"><img class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="413" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="620" decoding="async" height="167" id="mwYw" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Ph15-071316.jpg" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/71/Ph15-071316.jpg/250px-Ph15-071316.jpg" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/71/Ph15-071316.jpg/500px-Ph15-071316.jpg 2x" width="250"/></a><figcaption id="mwZA">The 2022 election decided the successor of both President <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rodrigo_Duterte" id="mwZQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rodrigo Duterte">Rodrigo Duterte</a> and Vice President <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leni_Robredo" id="mwZg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leni Robredo">Leni Robredo</a>, whose terms as President and Vice President expired at noon on June 30, 2022; respectively. This photo shows Duterte inducting Robredo to head the <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hudcc" id="mwZw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Hudcc">HUDCC</a>.</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mwaA">In the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2016_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwaQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2016 Philippine presidential election">2016 presidential and vice presidential elections</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_City" id="mwag" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao City">Davao City</a> Mayor <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rodrigo_Duterte" id="mwaw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rodrigo Duterte">Rodrigo Duterte</a> of Partido Demokratiko Pilipino–Lakas ng Bayan (<abbr about="#mwt67" data-mw='{"attribs":[[{"txt":"title"},{"html":"&lt;span typeof=\"mw:Nowiki\" id=\"mwbA\"&gt;translation&lt;/span&gt;"}]],"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Translation","href":"./Template:Translation"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Philippine Democratic Party–People Power"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwbQ" title="translation" typeof="mw:Transclusion mw:ExpandedAttrs">transl.</abbr><span about="#mwt67" id="mwbg"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>Philippine Democratic Party–People Power</span>; <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PDP–Laban" id="mwbw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="PDP–Laban">PDP–Laban</a>) won the presidency against four other candidates, while <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/House_of_Representatives_of_the_Philippines" id="mwcA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="House of Representatives of the Philippines">House</a> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camarines_Sur's_3rd_congressional_district" id="mwcQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Camarines Sur's 3rd congressional district">representative</a> from <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camarines_Sur" id="mwcg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Camarines Sur">Camarines Sur</a> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leni_Robredo" id="mwcw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leni Robredo">Leni Robredo</a> of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liberal_Party_(Philippines)" id="mwdA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Liberal Party (Philippines)">Liberal Party</a> won against Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwdQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a> and four others in the closest vice-presidential election <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1965_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwdg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1965 Philippine presidential election">since 1965</a>. Marcos put the result under protest in the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Presidential_Electoral_Tribunal" id="mwdw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Presidential Electoral Tribunal">Presidential Electoral Tribunal</a>.<sup about="#mwt72" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-13"}}' id="cite_ref-13" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-13" id="mweA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mweQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mweg">[</span>12<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwew">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwfA">In October 2019, the Presidential Electoral Tribunal released its report on Marcos's electoral protest against Robredo on Marcos's pilot provinces of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camarines_Sur" id="mwfQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Camarines Sur">Camarines Sur</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iloilo" id="mwfg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Iloilo">Iloilo</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Negros_Oriental" id="mwfw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Negros Oriental">Negros Oriental</a>, and showed that Robredo  had increased her lead by 15,742 votes. The tribunal voted to defer making a decision on the protest and instead proceeded with Marcos's plea to nullify the votes from the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Autonomous_Region_in_Muslim_Mindanao" id="mwgA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao">Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao</a> (ARMM) provinces of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basilan" id="mwgQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Basilan">Basilan</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lanao_del_Sur" id="mwgg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Lanao del Sur">Lanao del Sur</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maguindanao" id="mwgw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Maguindanao">Maguindanao</a>.<sup about="#mwt76" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":28"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:28-14"}}' id="cite_ref-:28_14-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:28-14" id="mwhA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwhQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwhg">[</span>13<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwhw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwiA">Those who dissented from the decision said that the protest should have been dismissed, as Marcos had failed to recover votes from his 3 pilot provinces, citing the rules of the tribunal. They were overruled when the others said that Marcos's plea on the ARMM provinces should also be resolved.<sup about="#mwt77" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":28"}}' id="cite_ref-:28_14-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:28-14" id="mwiQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwig"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwiw">[</span>13<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwjA">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwjQ">In July 2016, Vice President Robredo was appointed to head the <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Housing_and_Urban_Development_Coordinating_Council_(Philippines)" id="mwjg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Housing and Urban Development Coordinating Council (Philippines)">Housing and Urban Development Coordinating Council</a> (HUDCC),<sup about="#mwt81" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-15"}}' id="cite_ref-15" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-15" id="mwjw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwkA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwkQ">[</span>14<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwkg">]</span></span></a></sup> but resigned in December 2016 after being told to stop attending all cabinet meetings, amid her criticism of the administration's <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philippine_drug_war" id="mwkw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Philippine drug war">war on drugs</a>.<sup about="#mwt85" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-16"}}' id="cite_ref-16" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-16" id="mwlA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwlQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwlg">[</span>15<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwlw">]</span></span></a></sup> The president later told his allies to stop trying to impeach Robredo.<sup about="#mwt89" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-17"}}' id="cite_ref-17" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-17" id="mwmA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwmQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwmg">[</span>16<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwmw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwnA">In the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/17th_Congress_of_the_Philippines" id="mwnQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="17th Congress of the Philippines">17th Congress</a>, Representative <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pantaleon_Alvarez" id="mwng" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pantaleon Alvarez">Pantaleon Alvarez</a> from <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_del_Norte" id="mwnw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao del Norte">Davao del Norte</a> was elected <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speaker_of_the_House_of_Representatives_of_the_Philippines" id="mwoA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Speaker of the House of Representatives of the Philippines">Speaker of the House of Representatives</a> in July 2016.<sup about="#mwt95" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-18"}}' id="cite_ref-18" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-18" id="mwoQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwog"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwow">[</span>17<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwpA">]</span></span></a></sup> Midway during the 17th Congress, former President <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gloria_Macapagal_Arroyo" id="mwpQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Gloria Macapagal Arroyo">Gloria Macapagal Arroyo</a>, who was the representative from <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pampanga" id="mwpg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pampanga">Pampanga</a>, ousted Alvarez from the speakership. The ouster was reportedly due to a dispute between Alvarez and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mayor_of_Davao_City" id="mwpw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mayor of Davao City">Davao City Mayor</a> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sara_Duterte" id="mwqA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sara Duterte">Sara Duterte</a>, the president's daughter, when she branded him as being from the opposition when she launched the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hugpong_ng_Pagbabago" id="mwqQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Hugpong ng Pagbabago">Hugpong ng Pagbabago</a> (<abbr about="#mwt90" data-mw='{"attribs":[[{"txt":"title"},{"html":"&lt;span typeof=\"mw:Nowiki\" id=\"mwqg\"&gt;translation&lt;/span&gt;"}]],"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Translation","href":"./Template:Translation"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Alliance for Change"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwqw" title="translation" typeof="mw:Transclusion mw:ExpandedAttrs">transl.</abbr><span about="#mwt90" id="mwrA"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>Alliance for Change</span>; HNP), a regional political party in the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_Region" id="mwrQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao Region">Davao Region</a> of which both <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_del_Norte" id="mwrg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao del Norte">Davao del Norte</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_City" id="mwrw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao City">Davao City</a> are a part.<sup about="#mwt99" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-19"}}' id="cite_ref-19" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-19" id="mwsA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwsQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwsg">[</span>18<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwsw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwtA">In the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2019_Philippine_Senate_election" id="mwtQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2019 Philippine Senate election">2019 midterm Senate election</a>, the opposition to Duterte fielded the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Otso_Diretso" id="mwtg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Otso Diretso">Otso Diretso</a> (<abbr about="#mwt100" data-mw='{"attribs":[[{"txt":"title"},{"html":"&lt;span typeof=\"mw:Nowiki\" id=\"mwtw\"&gt;translation&lt;/span&gt;"}]],"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Translation","href":"./Template:Translation"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Straight Eight"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwuA" title="translation" typeof="mw:Transclusion mw:ExpandedAttrs">transl.</abbr><span about="#mwt100" id="mwuQ"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>Straight Eight</span>) coalition,<sup about="#mwt105" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-20"}}' id="cite_ref-20" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-20" id="mwug"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwuw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwvA">[</span>19<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwvQ">]</span></span></a></sup> while the administration fielded its own slate under the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hugpong_ng_Pagbabago" id="mwvg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Hugpong ng Pagbabago">Hugpong ng Pagbabago</a> banner.<sup about="#mwt108" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-21"}}' id="cite_ref-21" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-21" id="mwvw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwwA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwwQ">[</span>20<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwwg">]</span></span></a></sup> Hugpong won 9 of the 12 seats contested, while Otso Diretso won no seats. <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cynthia_Villar" id="mwww" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cynthia Villar">Cynthia Villar</a>, wife of defeated <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2010_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwxA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2010 Philippine presidential election">2010 presidential</a> candidate <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manny_Villar" id="mwxQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Manny Villar">Manny Villar</a>, topped the election, while <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2013_Philippine_Senate_election" id="mwxg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2013 Philippine Senate election">2013's Senate election topnotcher</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2016_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwxw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2016 Philippine presidential election">2016 defeated presidential</a> candidate <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Poe" id="mwyA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Grace Poe">Grace Poe</a> finished second.<sup about="#mwt112" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-22"}}' id="cite_ref-22" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-22" id="mwyQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwyg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwyw">[</span>21<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwzA">]</span></span></a></sup> While Sara Duterte's Hugpong won in the Davao Region, they notably failed to unseat Alvarez, and PDP–Laban defeated Hugpong's candidate for governor of Davao del Norte, and won both of the province's seats in the House of Representatives.<sup about="#mwt116" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-23"}}' id="cite_ref-23" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-23" id="mwzQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwzg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwzw">[</span>22<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw0A">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mw0Q">Administration-allied parties also won in the House of Representatives, but there was a dispute on who should be Speaker. President Duterte brokered a term-sharing agreement between <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alan_Peter_Cayetano" id="mw0g" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Alan Peter Cayetano">Alan Peter Cayetano</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lord_Allan_Velasco" id="mw0w" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Lord Allan Velasco">Lord Allan Velasco</a>, with the former serving the first 15 months of the term and Velasco the final 18 months.<sup about="#mwt120" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"pna2"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-pna2-24"}}' id="cite_ref-pna2_24-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-pna2-24" id="mw1A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw1Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw1g">[</span>23<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw1w">]</span></span></a></sup> Meanwhile, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tito_Sotto" id="mw2A" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Tito Sotto">Tito Sotto</a> was re-elected as Senate President.<sup about="#mwt124" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-25"}}' id="cite_ref-25" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-25" id="mw2Q"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw2g"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw2w">[</span>24<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw3A">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mw3Q">In November 2019, the president challenged Robredo to co-chair the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inter-Agency_Committee_on_Anti-Illegal_Drugs" id="mw3g" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Inter-Agency Committee on Anti-Illegal Drugs">Inter-Agency Committee on Anti-Illegal Drugs</a> (ICAD), the office that oversees the war on drugs, along with the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philippine_Drug_Enforcement_Agency" id="mw3w" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency">Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency</a> chief, which she accepted.<sup about="#mwt128" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-26"}}' id="cite_ref-26" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-26" id="mw4A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw4Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw4g">[</span>25<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw4w">]</span></span></a></sup> A week later, Duterte said that he would fire Robredo if she shared state secrets about the drug war.<sup about="#mwt132" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-27"}}' id="cite_ref-27" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-27" id="mw5A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw5Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw5g">[</span>26<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw5w">]</span></span></a></sup> Several days later, Duterte said he could not trust Robredo after she asked the government for a list of high-value targets in the drug war.<sup about="#mwt136" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-28"}}' id="cite_ref-28" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-28" id="mw6A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw6Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw6g">[</span>27<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw6w">]</span></span></a></sup> Robredo replied, "He should tell me straight if he wants me out."<sup about="#mwt140" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-29"}}' id="cite_ref-29" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-29" id="mw7A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw7Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw7g">[</span>28<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw7w">]</span></span></a></sup> A day later, Duterte fired Robredo as co-chair of the ICAD.<sup about="#mwt144" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-30"}}' id="cite_ref-30" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-30" id="mw8A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw8Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw8g">[</span>29<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw8w">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mw9A">During the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic_in_the_Philippines" id="mw9Q" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="COVID-19 pandemic in the Philippines">COVID-19 pandemic in the Philippines</a>, the government was criticized by the opposition for <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philippine_government_response_to_the_COVID-19_pandemic" id="mw9g" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Philippine government response to the COVID-19 pandemic">its response to the pandemic</a>. Robredo had a televised speech in August claiming that the government had no plans on how to resolve the pandemic and shared ten recommendations on how to resolve it.<sup about="#mwt148" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-31"}}' id="cite_ref-31" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-31" id="mw9w"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw-A"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw-Q">[</span>30<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw-g">]</span></span></a></sup> Duterte himself, in a separate speech days later, asked Robredo not to "add fuel to the fire".<sup about="#mwt152" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-32"}}' id="cite_ref-32" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-32" id="mw-w"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mw_A"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mw_Q">[</span>31<span class="cite-bracket" id="mw_g">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mw_w">In June 2020, American boxing promoter <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bob_Arum" id="mwAQA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bob Arum">Bob Arum</a> said that Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manny_Pacquiao" id="mwAQE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Manny Pacquiao">Manny Pacquiao</a> had confided to him that he would run for president in 2022.<sup about="#mwt155" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":0a"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:0a-33"}}' id="cite_ref-:0a_33-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:0a-33" id="mwAQI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAQM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAQQ">[</span>32<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAQU">]</span></span></a></sup> Pacquiao later denied talking about politics with Arum.<sup about="#mwt158" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":1b"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:1b-34"}}' id="cite_ref-:1b_34-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:1b-34" id="mwAQY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAQc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAQg">[</span>33<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAQk">]</span></span></a></sup> On December 3, 2020, Pacquiao was elected party president of PDP–Laban, of which President Duterte is the party chairman.<sup about="#mwt161" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-35"}}' id="cite_ref-35" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-35" id="mwAQo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAQs"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAQw">[</span>34<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAQ0">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt164" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-36"}}' id="cite_ref-36" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-36" id="mwAQ4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAQ8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwARA">[</span>35<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwARE">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt167" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-37"}}' id="cite_ref-37" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-37" id="mwARI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwARM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwARQ">[</span>36<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwARU">]</span></span></a></sup> On May 3, 2021, when Pacquiao was asked about his intentions of running for president, he replied "for now, let's not think about that."<sup about="#mwt170" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-38"}}' id="cite_ref-38" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-38" id="mwARY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwARc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwARg">[</span>37<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwARk">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwARo">In March 2021, former Supreme Court Associate Justice <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antonio_Carpio" id="mwARs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Antonio Carpio">Antonio Carpio</a> launched "1Sambayan" (read as "isambayan", meaning "one nation"), a coalition that aimed to place one lone candidate against Duterte's endorsed successor.<sup about="#mwt174" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-39"}}' id="cite_ref-39" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-39" id="mwARw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAR0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAR4">[</span>38<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAR8">]</span></span></a></sup> Carpio aimed to avoid <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vote_splitting" id="mwASA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Vote splitting">Vote splitting</a>, pointing out that Duterte had won over <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mar_Roxas" id="mwASE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mar Roxas">Mar Roxas</a> and Grace Poe in 2016 as those who were against him were not united.<sup about="#mwt178" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-40"}}' id="cite_ref-40" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-40" id="mwASI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwASM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwASQ">[</span>39<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwASU">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwASY">1Sambayan considered Robredo, Moreno, Poe, and Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nancy_Binay" id="mwASc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Nancy Binay">Nancy Binay</a> as candidates.<sup about="#mwt182" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Charm"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Charm-41"}}' id="cite_ref-Charm_41-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Charm-41" id="mwASg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwASk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwASo">[</span>40<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwASs">]</span></span></a></sup> Both Moreno and Pacquiao, with the former being one of 1Sambayan's choices, stated that they did not want to talk about politics during the pandemic.<sup about="#mwt185" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-42"}}' id="cite_ref-42" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-42" id="mwASw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAS0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAS4">[</span>41<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAS8">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt189" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-43"}}' id="cite_ref-43" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-43" id="mwATA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwATE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwATI">[</span>42<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwATM">]</span></span></a></sup> Former senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antonio_Trillanes" id="mwATQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Antonio Trillanes">Antonio Trillanes IV</a> said that there was never an offer from 1Sambayan for him to run, but if that Robredo did not seek the presidency in 2022, he would consider entering the presidential race.<sup about="#mwt193" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-44"}}' id="cite_ref-44" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-44" id="mwATU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwATY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwATc">[</span>43<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwATg">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwATk">A <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2021_PDP–Laban_dispute" id="mwATo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2021 PDP–Laban dispute">dispute</a> within the PDP–Laban began on March 12, 2021<sup about="#mwt196" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-45"}}' id="cite_ref-45" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-45" id="mwATs"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwATw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAT0">[</span>44<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAT4">]</span></span></a></sup> when Senator Pacquiao began to criticize President Duterte and the government regarding the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Territorial_disputes_in_the_South_China_Sea" id="mwAT8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Territorial disputes in the South China Sea">dispute in the South China Sea</a>, alleged corruption in the government agencies under the Duterte administration,<sup about="#mwt199" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-46"}}' id="cite_ref-46" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-46" id="mwAUA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAUE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAUI">[</span>45<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAUM">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt202" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-47"}}' id="cite_ref-47" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-47" id="mwAUQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAUU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAUY">[</span>46<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAUc">]</span></span></a></sup> the government handling of the COVID-19 pandemic, and the endorsement of candidates for the 2022 presidential election, leading to the creation of two factions within the party.<sup about="#mwt205" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"pacquiaodutertebfkue"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-pacquiaodutertebfkue-48"}}' id="cite_ref-pacquiaodutertebfkue_48-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-pacquiaodutertebfkue-48" id="mwAUg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAUk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAUo">[</span>47<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAUs">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAUw">On March 23, 2021, Senate President Tito Sotto stated that he and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panfilo_Lacson" id="mwAU0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Panfilo Lacson">Panfilo Lacson</a> were being pushed to form a tandem. Sotto said both of them were yet to be convinced on this, and when asked if he would run for higher office in 2022, he said that he had not yet made a decision.<sup about="#mwt209" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-49"}}' id="cite_ref-49" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-49" id="mwAU4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAU8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAVA">[</span>48<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAVE">]</span></span></a></sup> Lacson later declined the offer from 1Sambayan in a letter to Justice Carpio, in which he cited his principal sponsorship of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anti-Terrorism_Act_of_2020" id="mwAVI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Anti-Terrorism Act of 2020">Anti-Terrorism Law</a> as "inconsistent and incongruent" to the goals of the coalition.<sup about="#mwt213" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-50"}}' id="cite_ref-50" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-50" id="mwAVM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAVQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAVU">[</span>49<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAVY">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAVc">On June 12, 2021, 1Sambayan announced their six initial nominees to go through the coalition's selection process for a presidential and vice-presidential tandem. The nominees (whom members of the coalition may vote for as their candidate for either post) included: senator Poe, vice president Robredo, former Senator Trillanes, human rights lawyer and former senatorial candidate <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chel_Diokno" id="mwAVg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Chel Diokno">Chel Diokno</a>, incumbent <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deputy_Speaker_of_the_House_of_Representatives_of_the_Philippines" id="mwAVk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Deputy Speaker of the House of Representatives of the Philippines">house deputy speaker</a> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vilma_Santos" id="mwAVo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Vilma Santos">Vilma Santos-Recto</a>, and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Citizens'_Battle_Against_Corruption" id="mwAVs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Citizens' Battle Against Corruption">CIBAC</a> representative and house deputy speaker <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eddie_Villanueva" id="mwAVw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Eddie Villanueva">Eddie Villanueva</a>.<sup about="#mwt217" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-51"}}' id="cite_ref-51" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-51" id="mwAV0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAV4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAV8">[</span>50<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAWA">]</span></span></a></sup> Moreno and Binay declined 1Sambayan's offer to run under their coalition.<sup about="#mwt221" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"rappler.com"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-rappler.com-52"}}' id="cite_ref-rappler.com_52-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-rappler.com-52" id="mwAWE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAWI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAWM">[</span>51<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAWQ">]</span></span></a></sup> Soon after, Senator Poe and Congressman Santos also withdrew their names from contention.<sup about="#mwt225" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-53"}}' id="cite_ref-53" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-53" id="mwAWU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAWY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAWc">[</span>52<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAWg">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt228" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Vilma no"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Vilma_no-54"}}' id="cite_ref-Vilma_no_54-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Vilma_no-54" id="mwAWk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAWo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAWs">[</span>53<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAWw">]</span></span></a></sup> At the same time, Vice President Robredo, former Senator Trillanes, and Congressman Villanueva expressed their support for unity in 2022 under 1Sambayan.<sup about="#mwt232" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-55"}}' id="cite_ref-55" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-55" id="mwAW0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAW4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAW8">[</span>54<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAXA">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAXE">Lacson and Sotto formally announced their candidacies on July, with their campaign to be launched in August.<sup about="#mwt237" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"lacson-sotto2"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-lacson-sotto2-56"}}' id="cite_ref-lacson-sotto2_56-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-lacson-sotto2-56" id="mwAXI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAXM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAXQ">[</span>55<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAXU">]</span></span></a></sup> Lacson was later sworn in as a member of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Partido_para_sa_Demokratikong_Reporma" id="mwAXY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Partido para sa Demokratikong Reporma">Partido para sa Demokratikong Reporma</a> (<abbr about="#mwt233" data-mw='{"attribs":[[{"txt":"title"},{"html":"&lt;span typeof=\"mw:Nowiki\" id=\"mwAXc\"&gt;translation&lt;/span&gt;"}]],"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Translation","href":"./Template:Translation"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Party for Democratic Reforms"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwAXg" title="translation" typeof="mw:Transclusion mw:ExpandedAttrs">transl.</abbr><span about="#mwt233" id="mwAXk"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>Party for Democratic Reforms</span>), the party of former secretary of Defense <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Renato_de_Villa" id="mwAXo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Renato de Villa">Renato de Villa</a> who lost the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1998_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwAXs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1998 Philippine presidential election">1998 presidential election</a>.<sup about="#mwt241" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-57"}}' id="cite_ref-57" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-57" id="mwAXw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAX0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAX4">[</span>56<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAX8">]</span></span></a></sup> On September 8, Lacson and Sotto formally launched their tandem via social media, in a taped production in an undisclosed studio.<sup about="#mwt244" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-58"}}' id="cite_ref-58" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-58" id="mwAYA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAYE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAYI">[</span>57<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAYM">]</span></span></a></sup> The tandem filed their candidacies on October 6.<sup about="#mwt247" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-59"}}' id="cite_ref-59" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-59" id="mwAYQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAYU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAYY">[</span>58<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAYc">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAYg"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isko_Moreno" id="mwAYk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Isko Moreno">Isko Moreno</a>, who had returned to the political limelight after being elected <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mayor_of_Manila" id="mwAYo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mayor of Manila">Mayor of Manila</a>, was expected to take his oath as a member of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aksyon_Demokratiko" id="mwAYs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Aksyon Demokratiko">Aksyon Demokratiko</a> (<abbr about="#mwt248" data-mw='{"attribs":[[{"txt":"title"},{"html":"&lt;span typeof=\"mw:Nowiki\" id=\"mwAYw\"&gt;translation&lt;/span&gt;"}]],"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Translation","href":"./Template:Translation"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Democratic Action"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwAY0" title="translation" typeof="mw:Transclusion mw:ExpandedAttrs">transl.</abbr><span about="#mwt248" id="mwAY4"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>Democratic Action</span>), the party founded by Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raul_Roco" id="mwAY8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Raul Roco">Raul Roco</a><sup about="#mwt253" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-60"}}' id="cite_ref-60" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-60" id="mwAZA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAZE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAZI">[</span>59<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAZM">]</span></span></a></sup> after resigning from the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Unity_Party_(Philippines)" id="mwAZQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="National Unity Party (Philippines)">National Unity Party</a>.<sup about="#mwt257" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-61"}}' id="cite_ref-61" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-61" id="mwAZU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAZY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAZc">[</span>60<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAZg">]</span></span></a></sup> However, this did not happen as an unexpectedly large number of people turned up on vaccination sites, particularly in Manila.<sup about="#mwt261" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-62"}}' id="cite_ref-62" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-62" id="mwAZk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAZo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAZs">[</span>61<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAZw">]</span></span></a></sup> Moreno was subsequently elected party president a week later.<sup about="#mwt265" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"cnnphilippines.com"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-cnnphilippines.com-63"}}' id="cite_ref-cnnphilippines.com_63-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-cnnphilippines.com-63" id="mwAZ0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAZ4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAZ8">[</span>62<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAaA">]</span></span></a></sup> He later announced his presidential bid on September 22 with Dr. <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Willie_Ong" id="mwAaE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Willie Ong">Willie Ong</a> as his running mate.<sup about="#mwt266" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"isko ong2"}}' id="cite_ref-isko_ong2_64-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-isko_ong2-64" id="mwAaI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAaM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAaQ">[</span>63<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAaU">]</span></span></a></sup> They filed their certificates of candidacy (COCs) on October 4.<sup about="#mwt270" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"isko"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-isko-65"}}' id="cite_ref-isko_65-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-isko-65" id="mwAaY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAac"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAag">[</span>64<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAak">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAao">On September 30, 2021, 1Sambayan nominated Robredo as their standard bearer.  On October 7, Robredo accepted the nomination and announced that she would run for President.<sup about="#mwt271" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":7"}}' id="cite_ref-:7_66-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:7-66" id="mwAas"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAaw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAa0">[</span>65<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAa4">]</span></span></a></sup> She later filed her certificate of candidacy on the same day as an independent.<sup about="#mwt272" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":8"}}' id="cite_ref-:8_67-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:8-67" id="mwAa8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAbA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAbE">[</span>66<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAbI">]</span></span></a></sup> Robredo explained that she was running as an independent to show that she is open to making alliances.<sup about="#mwt273" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":9"}}' id="cite_ref-:9_68-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:9-68" id="mwAbM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAbQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAbU">[</span>67<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAbY">]</span></span></a></sup> After Vice-President Robredo announced her presidential candidacy, several sources from the Liberal Party indicated that Senator <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Francis_Pangilinan" id="mwAbc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Francis Pangilinan">Francis Pangilinan</a> would be her running mate for her presidential bid.<sup about="#mwt277" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":10"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:10-69"}}' id="cite_ref-:10_69-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:10-69" id="mwAbg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAbk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAbo">[</span>68<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAbs">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt281" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":11"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:11-70"}}' id="cite_ref-:11_70-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:11-70" id="mwAbw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAb0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAb4">[</span>69<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAb8">]</span></span></a></sup> Pangilinan filed his candidacy for vice president a day after Robredo.<sup about="#mwt285" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":12"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:12-71"}}' id="cite_ref-:12_71-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:12-71" id="mwAcA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAcE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAcI">[</span>70<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAcM">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAcQ">Back in January 2020, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwAcU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a> had confirmed that he would be running "for a national position" in 2022, although he did not specify which position.<sup about="#mwt292" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":13"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:13-72"}}' id="cite_ref-:13_72-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:13-72" id="mwAcY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAcc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAcg">[</span>71<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAck">]</span></span></a></sup> On September 21, 2021, the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Partido_Federal_ng_Pilipinas" id="mwAco" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Partido Federal ng Pilipinas">Partido Federal ng Pilipinas</a> (PFP; <abbr about="#mwt286" data-mw='{"attribs":[[{"txt":"title"},{"html":"&lt;span typeof=\"mw:Nowiki\" id=\"mwAcs\"&gt;translation&lt;/span&gt;"}]],"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Translation","href":"./Template:Translation"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Federal Party of the Philippines"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwAcw" title="translation" typeof="mw:Transclusion mw:ExpandedAttrs">transl.</abbr><span about="#mwt286" id="mwAc0"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>Federal Party of the Philippines</span>) nominated Marcos to run for president. During the national convention of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kilusang_Bagong_Lipunan" id="mwAc4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Kilusang Bagong Lipunan">Kilusang Bagong Lipunan</a> (KBL; <abbr about="#mwt288" data-mw='{"attribs":[[{"txt":"title"},{"html":"&lt;span typeof=\"mw:Nowiki\" id=\"mwAc8\"&gt;translation&lt;/span&gt;"}]],"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Translation","href":"./Template:Translation"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"New Society Movement"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwAdA" title="translation" typeof="mw:Transclusion mw:ExpandedAttrs">transl.</abbr><span about="#mwt288" id="mwAdE"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>New Society Movement</span>) in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Binangonan" id="mwAdI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Binangonan">Binangonan</a>, the party founded by his father, he had already been nominated as that party's candidate for president. Marcos, who was still a member of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nacionalista_Party" id="mwAdM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Nacionalista Party">Nacionalista Party</a>, thanked the KBL for the nomination, but said that he would announce his own plans "when the time comes."<sup about="#mwt296" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":15"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:15-73"}}' id="cite_ref-:15_73-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:15-73" id="mwAdQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAdU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAdY">[</span>72<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAdc">]</span></span></a></sup> On October 5, Marcos announced his presidential candidacy.<sup about="#mwt297" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":16"}}' id="cite_ref-:16_74-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:16-74" id="mwAdg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAdk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAdo">[</span>73<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAds">]</span></span></a></sup> Marcos then resigned from the Nacionalistas and was sworn in at the PFP chairman.<sup about="#mwt298" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":17"}}' id="cite_ref-:17_75-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:17-75" id="mwAdw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAd0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAd4">[</span>74<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAd8">]</span></span></a></sup> Marcos ultimately filed his presidential candidacy under the PFP on October 6.<sup about="#mwt299" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":18"}}' id="cite_ref-:18_76-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:18-76" id="mwAeA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAeE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAeI">[</span>75<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAeM">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAeQ">Davao City mayor Sara Duterte, despite being a frontrunner in early opinion polls, did not file a candidacy for a national position; instead she nominated for re-election in the Davao mayoral race, despite having previously stated on July 9, 2021, that she was open to running for president.<sup about="#mwt302" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":19"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:19-77"}}' id="cite_ref-:19_77-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:19-77" id="mwAeU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAeY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAec">[</span>76<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAeg">]</span></span></a></sup> On the final day for the filing of candidacies, Mayor Duterte did not show up; instead, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ronald_dela_Rosa" id="mwAek" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ronald dela Rosa">Ronald dela Rosa</a> filed his candidacy for president, stating that he was open to being substituted by Duterte.<sup about="#mwt305" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"whatifsara"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-whatifsara-78"}}' id="cite_ref-whatifsara_78-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-whatifsara-78" id="mwAeo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAes"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAew">[</span>77<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAe0">]</span></span></a></sup> Dela Rosa was running alongside Go, who had earlier filed his candidacy for the vice presidency on October 2, despite calls from the PDP-Laban Cusi wing for him to run for president.<sup about="#mwt308" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-79"}}' id="cite_ref-79" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-79" id="mwAe4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAe8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAfA">[</span>78<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAfE">]</span></span></a></sup> At the end of the period for the filing of the Certificates of Canvass (COCs), a total of 97 individuals had manifested their intention to run for President, and 29 did for Vice-President.<sup about="#mwt312" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":20"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:20-80"}}' id="cite_ref-:20_80-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:20-80" id="mwAfI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAfM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAfQ">[</span>79<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAfU">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAfY">On November 9, Duterte withdrew from the mayoral race in Davao. On November 11, she resigned from <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hugpong_ng_Pagbabago" id="mwAfc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Hugpong ng Pagbabago">Hugpong ng Pagbabago</a> and joined <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lakas–CMD" id="mwAfg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Lakas–CMD">Lakas–CMD</a> on the same day.<sup about="#mwt315" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":21"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:21-81"}}' id="cite_ref-:21_81-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:21-81" id="mwAfk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAfo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAfs">[</span>80<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAfw">]</span></span></a></sup> She filed her candidacy for the vice presidential post on November 13, 2021, substituting Lyle Fernando Uy.<sup about="#mwt318" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":22"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:22-82"}}' id="cite_ref-:22_82-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:22-82" id="mwAf0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAf4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAf8">[</span>81<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAgA">]</span></span></a></sup> Partido Federal ng Pilipinas adopted Duterte as their vice presidential candidate.<sup about="#mwt321" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":23"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:23-83"}}' id="cite_ref-:23_83-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:23-83" id="mwAgE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAgI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAgM">[</span>82<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAgQ">]</span></span></a></sup> Dela Rosa and Go both withdrew their candidacies on the same day.<sup about="#mwt324" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-84"}}' id="cite_ref-84" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-84" id="mwAgU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAgY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAgc">[</span>83<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAgg">]</span></span></a></sup> A few days later, Go launched his campaign for the presidential post.<sup about="#mwt327" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"duterte-vp"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-duterte-vp-85"}}' id="cite_ref-duterte-vp_85-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-duterte-vp-85" id="mwAgk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAgo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAgs">[</span>84<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAgw">]</span></span></a></sup> Go explained that he withdrew his bid to run for vice president, to avoid complicating Sara Duterte's vice presidential campaign. President Duterte was reported to be his running mate and was to file his candidacy on November 15,<sup about="#mwt330" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-86"}}' id="cite_ref-86" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-86" id="mwAg0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAg4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAg8">[</span>85<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAhA">]</span></span></a></sup> but filed for senator instead.<sup about="#mwt333" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-87"}}' id="cite_ref-87" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-87" id="mwAhE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAhI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAhM">[</span>86<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAhQ">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAhU">Upon filing his candidacy on November 15 via substitution, presidential aspirant and former <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Task_Force_to_End_Local_Communist_Armed_Conflict" id="mwAhY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="National Task Force to End Local Communist Armed Conflict">National Task Force to End Local Communist Armed Conflict</a> (NTF-ELCAC) spokesperson <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antonio_Parlade_Jr." id="mwAhc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Antonio Parlade Jr.">Antonio Parlade Jr.</a> accused fellow presidential aspirant Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bong_Go" id="mwAhg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bong Go">Bong Go</a> of controlling Duterte's decisions.<sup about="#mwt336" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-88"}}' id="cite_ref-88" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-88" id="mwAhk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAho"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAhs">[</span>87<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAhw">]</span></span></a></sup> Defense Secretary <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Delfin_Lorenzana" id="mwAh0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Delfin Lorenzana">Delfin Lorenzana</a> dismissed Parlade's claim, calling it baseless.<sup about="#mwt339" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-89"}}' id="cite_ref-89" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-89" id="mwAh4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAh8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAiA">[</span>88<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAiE">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAiI">On November 18, 2021, President Duterte claimed that an unnamed presidential aspirant from a well-known family was using <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cocaine" id="mwAiM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cocaine">cocaine</a> adding that the aspirant was a "weak leader" and "not a very strong leader, except for his name, the father."<sup about="#mwt342" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"dutcoke1"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-dutcoke1-90"}}' id="cite_ref-dutcoke1_90-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-dutcoke1-90" id="mwAiQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAiU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAiY">[</span>89<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAic">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt345" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-91"}}' id="cite_ref-91" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-91" id="mwAig"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAik"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAio">[</span>90<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAis">]</span></span></a></sup> The next day, presidential aspirants reacted to Duterte's statement. Leni Robredo and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leody_de_Guzman" id="mwAiw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leody de Guzman">Leody de Guzman</a> said that the government should file charges against the candidate allegedly using cocaine.<sup about="#mwt348" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-92"}}' id="cite_ref-92" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-92" id="mwAi0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAi4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAi8">[</span>91<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAjA">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt351" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"dutcoke2"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-dutcoke2-93"}}' id="cite_ref-dutcoke2_93-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-dutcoke2-93" id="mwAjE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAjI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAjM">[</span>92<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAjQ">]</span></span></a></sup> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isko_Moreno" id="mwAjU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Isko Moreno">Isko Moreno</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panfilo_Lacson" id="mwAjY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Panfilo Lacson">Panfilo Lacson</a> denied that the statement alluded to them.<sup about="#mwt352" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"dutcoke2"}}' id="cite_ref-dutcoke2_93-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-dutcoke2-93" id="mwAjc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAjg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAjk">[</span>92<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAjo">]</span></span></a></sup> A report speculated that his statement was alluded to Bongbong Marcos,<sup about="#mwt355" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-94"}}' id="cite_ref-94" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-94" id="mwAjs"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAjw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAj0">[</span>93<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAj4">]</span></span></a></sup> but Marcos's camp dismissed Duterte's claims.<sup about="#mwt358" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-95"}}' id="cite_ref-95" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-95" id="mwAj8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAkA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAkE">[</span>94<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAkI">]</span></span></a></sup> Lacson and Sotto, Marcos and Duterte, and Moreno and Ong all subsequently took drug tests and tested negative,<sup about="#mwt361" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-96"}}' id="cite_ref-96" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-96" id="mwAkM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAkQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAkU">[</span>95<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAkY">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt364" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-97"}}' id="cite_ref-97" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-97" id="mwAkc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAkg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAkk">[</span>96<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAko">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt367" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-98"}}' id="cite_ref-98" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-98" id="mwAks"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAkw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAk0">[</span>97<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAk4">]</span></span></a></sup> while Pacquiao presented a negative drug test from the Voluntary Anti-Doping Association.<sup about="#mwt370" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-99"}}' id="cite_ref-99" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-99" id="mwAk8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAlA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAlE">[</span>98<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAlI">]</span></span></a></sup> Robredo, Go, and de Guzman expressed their willingness to undergo a drug test.<sup about="#mwt371" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"dutcoke2"}}' id="cite_ref-dutcoke2_93-2" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-dutcoke2-93" id="mwAlM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAlQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAlU">[</span>92<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAlY">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt374" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-100"}}' id="cite_ref-100" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-100" id="mwAlc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAlg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAlk">[</span>99<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAlo">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt377" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-101"}}' id="cite_ref-101" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-101" id="mwAls"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAlw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAl0">[</span>100<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAl4">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
