<p id="mwAl8">On November 30, 2021, Go announced his intention to withdraw his candidacy for the presidency, without any substitute.<sup about="#mwt380" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-102"}}' id="cite_ref-102" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-102" id="mwAmA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAmE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAmI">[</span>101<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAmM">]</span></span></a></sup> He had earlier expressed his doubts about his presidential run on November 25.<sup about="#mwt383" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-103"}}' id="cite_ref-103" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-103" id="mwAmQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAmU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAmY">[</span>102<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAmc">]</span></span></a></sup> The COMELEC stated that Go had to personally file his statement of withdrawal,<sup about="#mwt386" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-104"}}' id="cite_ref-104" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-104" id="mwAmg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAmk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAmo">[</span>103<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAms">]</span></span></a></sup> which he did on December 14.<sup about="#mwt389" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":24"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:24-105"}}' id="cite_ref-:24_105-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:24-105" id="mwAmw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAm0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAm4">[</span>104<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAm8">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAnA">On January 17, 2022, the commission's second division dismissed the petition cancelling Marcos's candidacy. The petitioners cited Marcos's conviction on violating the National Internal Revenue Code when he had failed to file income tax returns in the early 1980s  during his tenure as governor of Ilocos Norte, which allegedly carried the punishment of perpetual disqualification from public office. The commission ruled that when the crime was committed, it did not carry the punishment cited by the petitioners, so Marcos did not misrepresent certificate of candidacy where he stated that he was not convicted of a crime of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moral_turpitude" id="mwAnE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Moral turpitude">moral turpitude</a>.<sup about="#mwt392" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-106"}}' id="cite_ref-106" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-106" id="mwAnI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAnM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAnQ">[</span>105<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAnU">]</span></span></a></sup> Marcos faced a separate disqualification case in the first division; its release of the decision was delayed when several of its staffers tested positive for <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COVID-19" id="mwAnY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="COVID-19">COVID-19</a>.<sup about="#mwt395" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-107"}}' id="cite_ref-107" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-107" id="mwAnc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAng"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAnk">[</span>106<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAno">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAns">On January 22, 2022, <i id="mwAnw">The Jessica Soho Presidential Interviews</i> premiered on <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/GMA_Network" id="mwAn0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="GMA Network">GMA</a>. Moreno, Robredo, Lacson, and Pacquiao participated in the interview,<sup about="#mwt398" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"jessicasoho1"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-jessicasoho1-108"}}' id="cite_ref-jessicasoho1_108-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-jessicasoho1-108" id="mwAn4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAn8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAoA">[</span>107<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAoE">]</span></span></a></sup> while Marcos refused to take part; his camp stated that Soho was "biased" against him.<sup about="#mwt401" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"js2"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-js2-109"}}' id="cite_ref-js2_109-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-js2-109" id="mwAoI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAoM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAoQ">[</span>108<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAoU">]</span></span></a></sup> GMA later responded to the allegation and refuted his camp's statement.<sup about="#mwt405" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"js3"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-js3-110"}}' id="cite_ref-js3_110-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-js3-110" id="mwAoY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAoc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAog">[</span>109<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAok">]</span></span></a></sup> In a separate interview in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/One_PH" id="mwAoo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="One PH">One PH</a>, Marcos equated bias with being "anti-Marcos," adding that it was useless as he was not going to answer issues about his father's presidency.<sup about="#mwt408" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-111"}}' id="cite_ref-111" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-111" id="mwAos"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAow"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAo0">[</span>110<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAo4">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAo8">On January 23, 2022, as part of its efforts against online disinformation, <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Twitter" id="mwApA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Twitter">Twitter</a> suspended more than 300 accounts. <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rappler" id="mwApE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rappler">Rappler</a> alleged that the accounts were linked to Marcos, which his campaign denied.<sup about="#mwt411" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-112"}}' id="cite_ref-112" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-112" id="mwApI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwApM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwApQ">[</span>111<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwApU">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Electoral_system" data-mw-section-id="2" id="mwApY"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Electoral_system">Electoral system</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=2" title="Edit section: Electoral system"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<figure class="mw-default-size" id="mwApc" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2022_Philippine_Elections_logo.svg" id="mwApg"><img class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="122" data-file-type="drawing" data-file-width="512" decoding="async" height="60" id="mwApk" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2022_Philippine_Elections_logo.svg" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/71/2022_Philippine_Elections_logo.svg/250px-2022_Philippine_Elections_logo.svg.png" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/71/2022_Philippine_Elections_logo.svg/500px-2022_Philippine_Elections_logo.svg.png 2x" width="250"/></a><figcaption id="mwApo">Logo for the 2022 NLE used for public materials and election awareness campaigns.</figcaption></figure>
<span about="#mwt412" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Presidential elections in the Philippines"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwAps" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt413" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt412" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwApw" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Presidential_elections_in_the_Philippines" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Presidential elections in the Philippines">Presidential elections in the Philippines</a></div>
<p id="mwAp0">According to the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Constitution_of_the_Philippines" id="mwAp4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Constitution of the Philippines">Constitution of the Philippines</a> of 1987, the election is held every six years after 1992, on the second Monday of May. The incumbent president is term-limited. The incumbent vice president may run for two consecutive terms. The <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/First-past-the-post_voting" id="mwAp8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="First-past-the-post voting">first-past-the-post voting</a> system is used to determine the winner: the candidate with the highest number of votes, whether or not one has a majority, wins the presidency.<sup about="#mwt416" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":27"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:27-113"}}' id="cite_ref-:27_113-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:27-113" id="mwAqA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAqE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAqI">[</span>112<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAqM">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwAqQ">The vice-presidential election is separate, is held on the same rules, and voters may split their ticket. According to the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Constitution_of_the_Philippines" id="mwAqU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Constitution of the Philippines">Constitution</a>, if two or more candidates get the most votes for either position, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congress_of_the_Philippines" id="mwAqY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Congress of the Philippines">Congress</a> shall vote from among them which shall be president or vice president, as the case may be.</p>
<p id="mwAqc">Both winners will serve six-year terms commencing at noon on June 30, 2022, and ending on the same day, six years later.<sup about="#mwt417" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":27"}}' id="cite_ref-:27_113-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:27-113" id="mwAqg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwAqk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAqo">[</span>112<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwAqs">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Tickets" data-mw-section-id="3" id="mwAqw"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Tickets">Tickets</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=3" title="Edit section: Tickets"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt418" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Candidates in the 2022 Philippine presidential election"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwAq0" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt419" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt418" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwAq4" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Candidates_in_the_2022_Philippine_presidential_election" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Candidates in the 2022 Philippine presidential election">Candidates in the 2022 Philippine presidential election</a></div>
<p id="mwAq8">The Commission on Elections released the official list of candidates on January 25, 2022,<sup about="#mwt422" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":31"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:31-5"}}' id="cite_ref-:31_5-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:31-5" id="mwArA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwArE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwArI">[</span>4<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwArM">]</span></span></a></sup> although it was finalized nine days before.<sup about="#mwt425" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-114"}}' id="cite_ref-114" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-114" id="mwArQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwArU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwArY">[</span>113<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwArc">]</span></span></a></sup> Candidates who appeared on the ballot could still have been disqualified until their proclamation.</p>
<p id="mwArg">The presidential election featured the most candidates since <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1992_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwArk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1992 Philippine presidential election">1992</a> with ten candidates running for president, while the vice presidential election featured the most candidates since <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1998_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwAro" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1998 Philippine presidential election">1998</a>, with nine candidates running for vice president. This is ordered by the surname of the presidential candidate.</p>
