</section><section aria-labelledby="Issues" data-mw-section-id="4" id="mwA9Q"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Issues">Issues</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=4" title="Edit section: Issues"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt470" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"See also","href":"./Template:See_also"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"2022 Philippine general election#Issues"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwA9U" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt471" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt470" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwA9Y" role="note">See also: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2022_Philippine_general_election#Issues" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2022 Philippine general election">2022 Philippine general election §<span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>Issues</a></div>
<p id="mwA9c">The election was held amidst the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic_in_the_Philippines" id="mwA9g" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="COVID-19 pandemic in the Philippines">COVID-19 pandemic</a>, which had severely damaged the country's economy and halted daily activities.<sup about="#mwt472" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"covidrecession"}}' id="cite_ref-covidrecession_2-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-covidrecession-2" id="mwA9k"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA9o"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA9s">[</span>1<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA9w">]</span></span></a></sup> Finance secretary <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carlos_Dominguez_III" id="mwA90" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Carlos Dominguez III">Carlos Dominguez III</a> predicted that the succeeding administration would face four main issues: debt management, inflation caused by global shortages, pandemic-induced inequalities, and climate change.<sup about="#mwt473" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":30"}}' id="cite_ref-:30_4-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:30-4" id="mwA94"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA98"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA-A">[</span>3<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA-E">]</span></span></a></sup> Other key issues included the continuation of President <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rodrigo_Duterte" id="mwA-I" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Rodrigo Duterte">Rodrigo Duterte</a>'s policies and a re-examination of the country's foreign relationships, particularly the United States, in light of its territorial dispute in the South China Sea with <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/China" id="mwA-M" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="China">China</a>.<sup about="#mwt474" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":29"}}' id="cite_ref-:29_3-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:29-3" id="mwA-Q"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA-U"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA-Y">[</span>2<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA-c">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwA-g">Former senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwA-k" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a>, despite not being endorsed but instead criticized by the outgoing President,<sup about="#mwt477" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-117"}}' id="cite_ref-117" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-117" id="mwA-o"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA-s"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA-w">[</span>114<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA-0">]</span></span></a></sup> promised broad continuity of his policies,<sup about="#mwt480" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-118"}}' id="cite_ref-118" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-118" id="mwA-4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA-8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA_A">[</span>115<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA_E">]</span></span></a></sup> vowing to promote foreign investment and continue infrastructure development alongside some of Duterte's programs.<sup about="#mwt483" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-119"}}' id="cite_ref-119" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-119" id="mwA_I"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA_M"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA_Q">[</span>116<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA_U">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt486" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-120"}}' id="cite_ref-120" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-120" id="mwA_Y"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA_c"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA_g">[</span>117<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA_k">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt489" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-121"}}' id="cite_ref-121" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-121" id="mwA_o"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA_s"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA_w">[</span>118<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwA_0">]</span></span></a></sup> He focused his messaging mainly on ushering unity among Filipinos.<sup about="#mwt492" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-122"}}' id="cite_ref-122" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-122" id="mwA_4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwA_8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBAA">[</span>119<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBAE">]</span></span></a></sup> Being the son and namesake of former president <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ferdinand_Marcos" id="mwBAI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ferdinand Marcos">Ferdinand Marcos</a>, Marcos's candidacy faced stiff opposition from various groups due to <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Martial_law_under_Ferdinand_Marcos" id="mwBAM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Martial law under Ferdinand Marcos">his father's regime</a>'s human rights abuses and widespread corruption during his tenure.<sup about="#mwt495" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-123"}}' id="cite_ref-123" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-123" id="mwBAQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBAU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBAY">[</span>120<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBAc">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt498" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-124"}}' id="cite_ref-124" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-124" id="mwBAg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBAk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBAo">[</span>121<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBAs">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBAw">Incumbent vice president <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leni_Robredo" id="mwBA0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leni Robredo">Leni Robredo</a> was considered from the outset to be Marcos's rival,<sup about="#mwt501" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-125"}}' id="cite_ref-125" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-125" id="mwBA4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBA8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBBA">[</span>122<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBBE">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt504" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-126"}}' id="cite_ref-126" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-126" id="mwBBI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBBM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBBQ">[</span>123<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBBU">]</span></span></a></sup> having won by a narrow margin against him in the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2016_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwBBY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2016 Philippine presidential election">2016 vice-presidential election</a>.<sup about="#mwt507" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-127"}}' id="cite_ref-127" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-127" id="mwBBc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBBg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBBk">[</span>124<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBBo">]</span></span></a></sup> Robredo, a critic of Duterte's policies,<sup about="#mwt510" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-128"}}' id="cite_ref-128" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-128" id="mwBBs"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBBw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBB0">[</span>125<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBB4">]</span></span></a></sup> offered a platform based on good governance and transparency.<sup about="#mwt513" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":26"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:26-129"}}' id="cite_ref-:26_129-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:26-129" id="mwBB8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBCA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBCE">[</span>126<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBCI">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt516" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-130"}}' id="cite_ref-130" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-130" id="mwBCM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBCQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBCU">[</span>127<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBCY">]</span></span></a></sup> She drew on her experience as a human rights lawyer and development worker.</p>
<p id="mwBCc">Meanwhile, Manila Mayor <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isko_Moreno" id="mwBCg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Isko Moreno">Isko Moreno</a> positioned himself as a <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Centrism" id="mwBCk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Centrism">centrist</a> alternative to both Marcos and Robredo,<sup about="#mwt519" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":0b"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:0b-131"}}' id="cite_ref-:0b_131-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:0b-131" id="mwBCo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBCs"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBCw">[</span>128<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBC0">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt522" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-132"}}' id="cite_ref-132" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-132" id="mwBC4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBC8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBDA">[</span>129<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBDE">]</span></span></a></sup> promising to make an "inclusive and open government" including many viewpoints.<sup about="#mwt525" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-133"}}' id="cite_ref-133" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-133" id="mwBDI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBDM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBDQ">[</span>130<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBDU">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt528" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-134"}}' id="cite_ref-134" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-134" id="mwBDY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBDc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBDg">[</span>131<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBDk">]</span></span></a></sup> He drew on his experience as Mayor, vowing to duplicate in the country what he had done in Manila.<sup about="#mwt531" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-135"}}' id="cite_ref-135" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-135" id="mwBDo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBDs"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBDw">[</span>132<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBD0">]</span></span></a></sup> Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manny_Pacquiao" id="mwBD4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Manny Pacquiao">Manny Pacquiao</a> ran on an anti-corruption platform and positioned himself as the candidate of the masses, pledging to initiate programs for the poor if elected.<sup about="#mwt534" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":14"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:14-136"}}' id="cite_ref-:14_136-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:14-136" id="mwBD8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBEA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBEE">[</span>133<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBEI">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBEM">Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panfilo_Lacson" id="mwBEQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Panfilo Lacson">Ping Lacson</a>, banking on his long tenure as a government official, campaigned on a law-and-order platform similar to Duterte's in 2016;<sup about="#mwt537" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-137"}}' id="cite_ref-137" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-137" id="mwBEU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBEY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBEc">[</span>134<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBEg">]</span></span></a></sup> his messaging was centered around restoring trust in the government.<sup about="#mwt540" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":3"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:3-138"}}' id="cite_ref-:3_138-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:3-138" id="mwBEk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBEo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBEs">[</span>135<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBEw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Presidential_election" data-mw-section-id="5" id="mwBE0"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Presidential_election">Presidential election</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=5" title="Edit section: Presidential election"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<section aria-labelledby="Candidates" data-mw-section-id="6" id="mwBE4"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Candidates">Candidates</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=6" title="Edit section: Candidates"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<section aria-labelledby="Ernesto_Abella_(Independent)" data-mw-section-id="7" id="mwBE8"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Ernesto_Abella_(Independent)"><span id="Ernesto_Abella_.28Independent.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Ernesto Abella (Independent)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=7" title="Edit section: Ernesto Abella (Independent)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwBFA"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ernesto_Abella" id="mwBFE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ernesto Abella">Ernesto Abella</a> said he was running since "ordinary people were being set aside", and that he was running as an independent since the ruling <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PDP–Laban" id="mwBFI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="PDP–Laban">PDP–Laban</a> did not choose him as their standard bearer.<sup about="#mwt543" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-139"}}' id="cite_ref-139" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-139" id="mwBFM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBFQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBFU">[</span>136<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBFY">]</span></span></a></sup> He later said that he was confident of not being declared as a <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nuisance_candidate" id="mwBFc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Nuisance candidate">nuisance candidate</a>.<sup about="#mwt546" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-140"}}' id="cite_ref-140" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-140" id="mwBFg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBFk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBFo">[</span>137<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBFs">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBFw">His platform was centered on three main pillars: "a fair society, a government that is felt by the people, and a strong nation." He promised to push for more <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public_participation" id="mwBF0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Public participation">public participation</a> in government affairs and to prioritize job creation and the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Agriculture_in_the_Philippines" id="mwBF4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Agriculture in the Philippines">agricultural sector in the country</a>.<sup about="#mwt547" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":1b"}}' id="cite_ref-:1b_34-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:1b-34" id="mwBF8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBGA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBGE">[</span>33<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBGI">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Leody_de_Guzman_(PLM)" data-mw-section-id="8" id="mwBGM"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Leody_de_Guzman_(PLM)"><span id="Leody_de_Guzman_.28PLM.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Leody de Guzman (PLM)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=8" title="Edit section: Leody de Guzman (PLM)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt548" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Leody de Guzman 2022 presidential campaign"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBGQ" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt549" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt548" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwBGU" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leody_de_Guzman_2022_presidential_campaign" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leody de Guzman 2022 presidential campaign">Leody de Guzman 2022 presidential campaign</a></div>
<p id="mwBGY"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leody_de_Guzman" id="mwBGc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leody de Guzman">Leody de Guzman</a>, chairman of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bukluran_ng_Manggagawang_Pilipino" id="mwBGg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bukluran ng Manggagawang Pilipino">Bukluran ng Manggagawang Pilipino</a>, a progressive labor group, ran unsuccessfully for Senator in 2019. De Guzman ran for president this time under the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Partido_Lakas_ng_Masa" id="mwBGk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Partido Lakas ng Masa">Partido Lakas ng Masa</a>.<sup about="#mwt553" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-141"}}' id="cite_ref-141" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-141" id="mwBGo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBGs"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBGw">[</span>138<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBG0">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBG4">According to his running mate, their vision for the country was <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Democratic_socialism" id="mwBG8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Democratic socialism">democratic socialism</a>. He ran on a platform based on labor and social issues. Some of his advocacies included repealing the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tax_Reform_for_Acceleration_and_Inclusion_Law" id="mwBHA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Tax Reform for Acceleration and Inclusion Law">TRAIN</a> and CREATE taxation laws as well as the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Anti-Terrorism_Act_of_2020" id="mwBHE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Anti-Terrorism Act of 2020">Anti-Terrorism Act of 2020</a>, pushing for the increase of the minimum wage, implementing a <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universal_basic_income" id="mwBHI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Universal basic income">universal basic income</a>, and legalizing <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Divorce" id="mwBHM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Divorce">divorce</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Same-sex_marriage" id="mwBHQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Same-sex marriage">same-sex marriage</a>, among others.<sup about="#mwt556" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-142"}}' id="cite_ref-142" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-142" id="mwBHU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBHY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBHc">[</span>139<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBHg">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Norberto_Gonzales_(PDSP)" data-mw-section-id="9" id="mwBHk"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Norberto_Gonzales_(PDSP)"><span id="Norberto_Gonzales_.28PDSP.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Norberto Gonzales (PDSP)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=9" title="Edit section: Norberto Gonzales (PDSP)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwBHo">Former <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Secretary_of_National_Defense_(Philippines)" id="mwBHs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Secretary of National Defense (Philippines)">secretary of National Defense</a> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Norberto_Gonzales" id="mwBHw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Norberto Gonzales">Norberto Gonzales</a> said that his campaign was a "new and more politically mature approach to winning the nation's heart and its consent to govern."<sup about="#mwt559" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-143"}}' id="cite_ref-143" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-143" id="mwBH0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBH4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBH8">[</span>140<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBIA">]</span></span></a></sup> He filed his candidacy on October 6, 2021, under the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Partido_Demokratiko_Sosyalista_ng_Pilipinas" id="mwBIE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Partido Demokratiko Sosyalista ng Pilipinas">Partido Demokratiko Sosyalista ng Pilipinas</a>. His platform focused on making significant changes in the country's government, such as making all <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Regions_of_the_Philippines" id="mwBII" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Regions of the Philippines">regions</a> autonomous and shifting from a <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Presidential_system" id="mwBIM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Presidential system">presidential system</a> to a <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parliamentary_system" id="mwBIQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Parliamentary system">parliamentary system</a>.<sup about="#mwt560" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":1bc"}}' id="cite_ref-:1bc_144-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:1bc-144" id="mwBIU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBIY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBIc">[</span>141<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBIg">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Panfilo_Lacson_(Independent)" data-mw-section-id="10" id="mwBIk"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Panfilo_Lacson_(Independent)"><span id="Panfilo_Lacson_.28Independent.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Panfilo Lacson (Independent)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=10" title="Edit section: Panfilo Lacson (Independent)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt561" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Panfilo Lacson 2022 presidential campaign"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBIo" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt562" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt561" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwBIs" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panfilo_Lacson_2022_presidential_campaign" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Panfilo Lacson 2022 presidential campaign">Panfilo Lacson 2022 presidential campaign</a></div>
<p id="mwBIw">In July 2021, Senate President Tito Sotto confirmed that <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panfilo_Lacson" id="mwBI0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Panfilo Lacson">Panfilo Lacson</a> would run for president in a tandem with him.<sup about="#mwt565" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"lacson-sotto"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-lacson-sotto-145"}}' id="cite_ref-lacson-sotto_145-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-lacson-sotto-145" id="mwBI4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBI8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBJA">[</span>142<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBJE">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt568" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"pingtito"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-pingtito-146"}}' id="cite_ref-pingtito_146-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-pingtito-146" id="mwBJI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBJM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBJQ">[</span>143<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBJU">]</span></span></a></sup> On September 8, the duo announced their candidacies for the upcoming election.<sup about="#mwt572" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"lacsonsotto"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-lacsonsotto-147"}}' id="cite_ref-lacsonsotto_147-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-lacsonsotto-147" id="mwBJY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBJc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBJg">[</span>144<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBJk">]</span></span></a></sup> He filed his candidacy under the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Partido_para_sa_Demokratikong_Reporma" id="mwBJo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Partido para sa Demokratikong Reporma">Partido para sa Demokratikong Reporma</a>. On March 24, 2022, Lacson resigned from Partido Reporma and became an independent candidate after the said party switched its endorsement to Robredo.<sup about="#mwt575" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"pingresigns"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-pingresigns-148"}}' id="cite_ref-pingresigns_148-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-pingresigns-148" id="mwBJs"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBJw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBJ0">[</span>145<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBJ4">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBJ8">If elected, he promised to restore trust in the government,<sup about="#mwt576" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":3"}}' id="cite_ref-:3_138-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:3-138" id="mwBKA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBKE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBKI">[</span>135<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBKM">]</span></span></a></sup> with a platform that revolveed around addressing corruption. He planned to initiate an anti-corruption drive, reform the national budget, and digitalize government services.<sup about="#mwt579" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-149"}}' id="cite_ref-149" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-149" id="mwBKQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBKU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBKY">[</span>146<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBKc">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Faisal_Mangondato_(Katipunan)" data-mw-section-id="11" id="mwBKg"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Faisal_Mangondato_(Katipunan)"><span id="Faisal_Mangondato_.28Katipunan.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Faisal Mangondato (Katipunan)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=11" title="Edit section: Faisal Mangondato (Katipunan)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwBKk"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Faisal_Mangondato" id="mwBKo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Faisal Mangondato">Faisal Mangondato</a>, who ran for Senator in 2019 and lost, filed to run for president on October 4 under the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Katipunan_ng_Kamalayang_Kayumanggi" id="mwBKs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Katipunan ng Kamalayang Kayumanggi">Katipunan ng Kamalayang Kayumanggi</a>.<sup about="#mwt583" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"filing4"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-filing4-150"}}' id="cite_ref-filing4_150-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-filing4-150" id="mwBKw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBK0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBK4">[</span>147<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBK8">]</span></span></a></sup> He planned to push for <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federalism_in_the_Philippines" id="mwBLA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Federalism in the Philippines">federalism in the Philippines</a>, proposing a <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Provisional_government" id="mwBLE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Provisional government">provisional government</a> for three years to complete the shift.<sup about="#mwt584" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":1bc"}}' id="cite_ref-:1bc_144-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:1bc-144" id="mwBLI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBLM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBLQ">[</span>141<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBLU">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Bongbong_Marcos_(PFP)" data-mw-section-id="12" id="mwBLY"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Bongbong_Marcos_(PFP)"><span id="Bongbong_Marcos_.28PFP.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Bongbong Marcos (PFP)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=12" title="Edit section: Bongbong Marcos (PFP)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt585" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Bongbong Marcos 2022 presidential campaign"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBLc" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt586" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt585" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwBLg" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos_2022_presidential_campaign" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos 2022 presidential campaign">Bongbong Marcos 2022 presidential campaign</a></div>
<p id="mwBLk">On October 5, 2021, Marcos announced his presidential candidacy.<sup about="#mwt590" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":16"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:16-74"}}' id="cite_ref-:16_74-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:16-74" id="mwBLo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBLs"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBLw">[</span>73<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBL0">]</span></span></a></sup> Initially nominated by four parties, Marcos chose to run under the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Partido_Federal_ng_Pilipinas" id="mwBL4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Partido Federal ng Pilipinas">Partido Federal ng Pilipinas</a>. He resigned from the Nacionalista Party and was sworn in as the PFP chairman the same day.<sup about="#mwt594" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":17"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:17-75"}}' id="cite_ref-:17_75-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:17-75" id="mwBL8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBMA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBME">[</span>74<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBMI">]</span></span></a></sup> Marcos ultimately filed his presidential candidacy under the PFP on October 6.<sup about="#mwt598" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":18"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:18-76"}}' id="cite_ref-:18_76-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:18-76" id="mwBMM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBMQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBMU">[</span>75<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBMY">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBMc">Marcos faced seven petitions against his bid.<sup about="#mwt601" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":2bb"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:2bb-151"}}' id="cite_ref-:2bb_151-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:2bb-151" id="mwBMg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBMk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBMo">[</span>148<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBMs">]</span></span></a></sup> The electoral commission dismissed the petition declaring him a nuisance candidate in early December.<sup about="#mwt604" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-152"}}' id="cite_ref-152" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-152" id="mwBMw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBM0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBM4">[</span>149<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBM8">]</span></span></a></sup> They also dismissed another petition which claims that the real Bongbong Marcos had died decades earlier, and that an impostor had taken his place.<sup about="#mwt607" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-153"}}' id="cite_ref-153" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-153" id="mwBNA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBNE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBNI">[</span>150<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBNM">]</span></span></a></sup> The consolidated disqualification case against him was dismissed on February 10, 2022, although one of the petitioners have filed a motion for reconsideration.<sup about="#mwt610" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-154"}}' id="cite_ref-154" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-154" id="mwBNQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBNU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBNY">[</span>151<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBNc">]</span></span></a></sup> The last petition against his candidacy was dismissed on April 20, 2022.<sup about="#mwt613" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-155"}}' id="cite_ref-155" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-155" id="mwBNg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBNk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBNo">[</span>152<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBNs">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBNw">His platform is centered on uniting the country,<sup about="#mwt616" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-156"}}' id="cite_ref-156" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-156" id="mwBN0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBN4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBN8">[</span>153<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBOA">]</span></span></a></sup> emphasizing the need for unity to recover from the pandemic.<sup about="#mwt619" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":112"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:112-157"}}' id="cite_ref-:112_157-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:112-157" id="mwBOE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBOI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBOM">[</span>154<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBOQ">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt622" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-158"}}' id="cite_ref-158" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-158" id="mwBOU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBOY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBOc">[</span>155<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBOg">]</span></span></a></sup> If elected, he promised to prioritize job creation,<sup about="#mwt625" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":192"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:192-159"}}' id="cite_ref-:192_159-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:192-159" id="mwBOk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBOo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBOs">[</span>156<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBOw">]</span></span></a></sup> infrastructure development,<sup about="#mwt628" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-160"}}' id="cite_ref-160" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-160" id="mwBO0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBO4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBO8">[</span>157<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBPA">]</span></span></a></sup> modernization of the agricultural and industrial sectors,<sup about="#mwt631" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-161"}}' id="cite_ref-161" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-161" id="mwBPE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBPI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBPM">[</span>158<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBPQ">]</span></span></a></sup> improving healthcare,<sup about="#mwt634" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":212"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:212-162"}}' id="cite_ref-:212_162-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:212-162" id="mwBPU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBPY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBPc">[</span>159<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBPg">]</span></span></a></sup> lower price of rice to ₱20.00 per kilogram and support for <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Small_and_medium-sized_enterprises" id="mwBPk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Small and medium-sized enterprises">small and medium-sized enterprises</a>. He also promised to continue President Duterte's policies.<sup about="#mwt637" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-163"}}' id="cite_ref-163" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-163" id="mwBPo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBPs"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBPw">[</span>160<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBP0">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Jose_Montemayor_Jr._(DPP)" data-mw-section-id="13" id="mwBP4"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Jose_Montemayor_Jr._(DPP)"><span id="Jose_Montemayor_Jr._.28DPP.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Jose Montemayor Jr. (DPP)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=13" title="Edit section: Jose Montemayor Jr. (DPP)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwBP8">Cardiologist <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jose_Montemayor_Jr." id="mwBQA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Jose Montemayor Jr.">Jose Montemayor Jr.</a> filed his candidacy on October 1, 2021, under the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Democratic_Party_of_the_Philippines" id="mwBQE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Democratic Party of the Philippines">Democratic Party of the Philippines</a>. He vowed to eliminate COVID-19, corruption, and criminality once elected.<sup about="#mwt640" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-164"}}' id="cite_ref-164" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-164" id="mwBQI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBQM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBQQ">[</span>161<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBQU">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Isko_Moreno_(Aksyon)" data-mw-section-id="14" id="mwBQY"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Isko_Moreno_(Aksyon)"><span id="Isko_Moreno_.28Aksyon.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Isko Moreno (Aksyon)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=14" title="Edit section: Isko Moreno (Aksyon)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt641" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Isko Moreno 2022 presidential campaign"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBQc" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt642" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt641" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwBQg" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isko_Moreno_2022_presidential_campaign" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Isko Moreno 2022 presidential campaign">Isko Moreno 2022 presidential campaign</a></div>
<p id="mwBQk">At the start of 2021, pollster <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pulse_Asia" id="mwBQo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pulse Asia">Pulse Asia</a> published an opinion poll showing <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isko_Moreno" id="mwBQs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Isko Moreno">Isko Moreno</a> in second place for president, and statistically tied for first with President Duterte in the vice presidential race.<sup about="#mwt645" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-165"}}' id="cite_ref-165" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-165" id="mwBQw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBQ0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBQ4">[</span>162<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBQ8">]</span></span></a></sup> The 1Sambayan convenors' group then included Moreno as one of the people they would consider choosing as its presidential candidate.<sup about="#mwt646" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Charm"}}' id="cite_ref-Charm_41-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Charm-41" id="mwBRA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBRE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBRI">[</span>40<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBRM">]</span></span></a></sup> Moreno begged off, as he was concentrating on his mayoral duties.<sup about="#mwt649" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-166"}}' id="cite_ref-166" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-166" id="mwBRQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBRU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBRY">[</span>163<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBRc">]</span></span></a></sup> By June, Moreno informed them that he was declining their offer.<sup about="#mwt650" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"rappler.com"}}' id="cite_ref-rappler.com_52-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-rappler.com-52" id="mwBRg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBRk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBRo">[</span>51<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBRs">]</span></span></a></sup> On September, Moreno was named president of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aksyon_Demokratiko" id="mwBRw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Aksyon Demokratiko">Aksyon Demokratiko</a>, the party founded by the late <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raul_Roco" id="mwBR0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Raul Roco">Raul Roco</a>.<sup about="#mwt651" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"cnnphilippines.com"}}' id="cite_ref-cnnphilippines.com_63-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-cnnphilippines.com-63" id="mwBR4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBR8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBSA">[</span>62<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBSE">]</span></span></a></sup> Moreno announced his candidacy on September 22 with Dr. <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Willie_Ong" id="mwBSI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Willie Ong">Willie Ong</a> as his running mate.<sup about="#mwt654" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"isko ong2"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-isko_ong2-64"}}' id="cite_ref-isko_ong2_64-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-isko_ong2-64" id="mwBSM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBSQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBSU">[</span>63<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBSY">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBSc">Some of Moreno proposed policies include maintaining the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Presidency_of_Rodrigo_Duterte" id="mwBSg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Presidency of Rodrigo Duterte">Duterte administration</a>'s "<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Build!_Build!_Build!" id="mwBSk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Build! Build! Build!">Build! Build! Build! Program</a>", additional <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public_housing" id="mwBSo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Public housing">public housing</a>, development of agriculture, tourism and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Creative_industries" id="mwBSs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Creative industries">creative industries</a>, strengthening of policies in healthcare and education, maintaining peaceful international relations, and ending divisive politics in favor of an "<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Open_government" id="mwBSw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Open government">open governance</a>" rooted in "competence" over "<a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Padrino_system" id="mwBS0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Padrino system">connections</a>".<sup about="#mwt657" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"agenda"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-agenda-167"}}' id="cite_ref-agenda_167-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-agenda-167" id="mwBS4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBS8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBTA">[</span>164<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBTE">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt660" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"pres"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-pres-168"}}' id="cite_ref-pres_168-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-pres-168" id="mwBTI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBTM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBTQ">[</span>165<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBTU">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt663" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-169"}}' id="cite_ref-169" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-169" id="mwBTY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBTc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBTg">[</span>166<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBTk">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Manny_Pacquiao_(PROMDI)" data-mw-section-id="15" id="mwBTo"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Manny_Pacquiao_(PROMDI)"><span id="Manny_Pacquiao_.28PROMDI.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Manny Pacquiao (PROMDI)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=15" title="Edit section: Manny Pacquiao (PROMDI)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt664" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Manny Pacquiao 2022 presidential campaign"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBTs" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt665" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt664" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwBTw" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manny_Pacquiao_2022_presidential_campaign" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Manny Pacquiao 2022 presidential campaign">Manny Pacquiao 2022 presidential campaign</a></div>
<p id="mwBT0">On September 19, Pacquiao accepted the nomination of the PDP-Laban faction led by Senator <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Koko_Pimentel" id="mwBT4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Koko Pimentel">Koko Pimentel</a>.<sup about="#mwt669" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-170"}}' id="cite_ref-170" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-170" id="mwBT8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBUA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBUE">[</span>167<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBUI">]</span></span></a></sup> On October 1, 2021, Pacquiao filed his certificate of candidacy for the presidency under <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PROMDI" id="mwBUM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="PROMDI">PROMDI</a>, the party founded by the late <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cebu" id="mwBUQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cebu">Cebu</a> governor <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lito_Osmeña" id="mwBUU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Lito Osmeña">Lito Osmeña</a>.<sup about="#mwt672" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-171"}}' id="cite_ref-171" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-171" id="mwBUY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBUc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBUg">[</span>168<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBUk">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBUo">His platform is centered around the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Poverty" id="mwBUs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Poverty">impoverished</a>. If elected, he plans to initiate housing programs for the poor, eliminating corruption, promote economic development, and work for healthcare reform.<sup about="#mwt673" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":14"}}' id="cite_ref-:14_136-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:14-136" id="mwBUw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBU0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBU4">[</span>133<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBU8">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Leni_Robredo_(Independent)" data-mw-section-id="16" id="mwBVA"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Leni_Robredo_(Independent)"><span id="Leni_Robredo_.28Independent.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Leni Robredo (Independent)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=16" title="Edit section: Leni Robredo (Independent)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt674" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Leni Robredo 2022 presidential campaign"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBVE" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt675" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt674" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwBVI" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leni_Robredo_2022_presidential_campaign" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leni Robredo 2022 presidential campaign">Leni Robredo 2022 presidential campaign</a></div>
<p id="mwBVM">On September 30, 2021, the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1Sambayan" id="mwBVQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1Sambayan">1Sambayan</a> coalition (the coalition of the opposition), nominated Robredo as their standard bearer.<sup about="#mwt679" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":2s"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:2s-172"}}' id="cite_ref-:2s_172-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:2s-172" id="mwBVU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBVY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBVc">[</span>169<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBVg">]</span></span></a></sup> According to <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Armin_Luistro" id="mwBVk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Armin Luistro">Armin Luistro</a>, one of the conveyors of 1Sambayan, Robredo accepted the nomination and said she would file her candidacy on October 5.<sup about="#mwt683" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":3b"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:3b-173"}}' id="cite_ref-:3b_173-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:3b-173" id="mwBVo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBVs"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBVw">[</span>170<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBV0">]</span></span></a></sup> Robredo's spokesman clarified that she had not reached a decision yet, but would finalize a decision on this before October 8.<sup about="#mwt686" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":4"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:4-174"}}' id="cite_ref-:4_174-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:4-174" id="mwBV4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBV8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBWA">[</span>171<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBWE">]</span></span></a></sup> On October 7, Robredo accepted the nomination and announced that she would run for president.<sup about="#mwt690" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":7"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:7-66"}}' id="cite_ref-:7_66-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:7-66" id="mwBWI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBWM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBWQ">[</span>65<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBWU">]</span></span></a></sup> She later filed her certificate of candidacy the same day as an independent.<sup about="#mwt694" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":8"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:8-67"}}' id="cite_ref-:8_67-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:8-67" id="mwBWY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBWc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBWg">[</span>66<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBWk">]</span></span></a></sup> Robredo explained that while she did not resign as chairperson of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liberal_Party_(Philippines)" id="mwBWo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Liberal Party (Philippines)">Liberal Party</a>, she was running as an independent to show that she was open to making alliances.<sup about="#mwt697" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":9"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:9-68"}}' id="cite_ref-:9_68-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:9-68" id="mwBWs"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBWw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBW0">[</span>67<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBW4">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBW8">Her platform focused on good governance and transparency.<sup about="#mwt698" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":26"}}' id="cite_ref-:26_129-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:26-129" id="mwBXA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBXE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBXI">[</span>126<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBXM">]</span></span></a></sup> She promised to prioritize healthcare and the economy. Some of her proposals included improving the quality of education in the country,<sup about="#mwt701" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-175"}}' id="cite_ref-175" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-175" id="mwBXQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBXU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBXY">[</span>172<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBXc">]</span></span></a></sup> developing the country's industries,<sup about="#mwt704" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-176"}}' id="cite_ref-176" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-176" id="mwBXg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBXk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBXo">[</span>173<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBXs">]</span></span></a></sup> supporting small and medium-sized enterprises,<sup about="#mwt707" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-177"}}' id="cite_ref-177" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-177" id="mwBXw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBX0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBX4">[</span>174<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBX8">]</span></span></a></sup> providing stable employment, strengthening the country's healthcare system,<sup about="#mwt710" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":62"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:62-178"}}' id="cite_ref-:62_178-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:62-178" id="mwBYA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBYE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBYI">[</span>175<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBYM">]</span></span></a></sup> and implementing more anti-corruption measures.<sup about="#mwt713" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-179"}}' id="cite_ref-179" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-179" id="mwBYQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBYU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBYY">[</span>176<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBYc">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section></section><section aria-labelledby="Debates_and_forums" data-mw-section-id="17" id="mwBYg"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Debates_and_forums">Debates and forums</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=17" title="Edit section: Debates and forums"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt714" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"2022 Philippine presidential debates"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBYk" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt715" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt714" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwBYo" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2022_Philippine_presidential_debates" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2022 Philippine presidential debates">2022 Philippine presidential debates</a></div>
<figure class="mw-default-size" id="mwBYs" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:IskoMorenoLeniRobredo2022Debate.jpg" id="mwBYw"><img class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="1171" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="1563" decoding="async" height="187" id="mwBY0" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:IskoMorenoLeniRobredo2022Debate.jpg" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1c/IskoMorenoLeniRobredo2022Debate.jpg/250px-IskoMorenoLeniRobredo2022Debate.jpg" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1c/IskoMorenoLeniRobredo2022Debate.jpg/500px-IskoMorenoLeniRobredo2022Debate.jpg 2x" width="250"/></a><figcaption id="mwBY4">From left to right: Presidential candidates <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manny_Pacquiao" id="mwBY8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Manny Pacquiao">Manny Pacquiao</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isko_Moreno" id="mwBZA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Isko Moreno">Isko Moreno</a>, and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leni_Robredo" id="mwBZE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leni Robredo">Leni Robredo</a> during the closing moments of the second <i id="mwBZI"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PiliPinas_Debates_2022" id="mwBZM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="PiliPinas Debates 2022">PiliPinas Debates 2022</a></i> on April 3, 2022.</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mwBZQ">The Commission on Elections (COMELEC) confirmed that they would organize debates for candidates running for both President and Vice-President. Unlike the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PiliPinas_Debates_2016" id="mwBZU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="PiliPinas Debates 2016">debates in the 2016 elections</a>, three presidential  and three vice-presidential debates would be held. Candidates would be required to follow safety protocols stated by <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inter-Agency_Task_Force_for_the_Management_of_Emerging_Infectious_Diseases" id="mwBZY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Inter-Agency Task Force for the Management of Emerging Infectious Diseases">Inter-Agency Task Force for the Management of Emerging Infectious Diseases</a> (IATF) with no audience participation due to the risks of the ongoing <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic" id="mwBZc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="COVID-19 pandemic">COVID-19 pandemic</a>.<sup about="#mwt718" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-180"}}' id="cite_ref-180" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-180" id="mwBZg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBZk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBZo">[</span>177<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBZs">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBZw">A few weeks later, the commission said that in addition to the three debates for each position, they would hold a primary debate, as a teaser of sorts for the upcoming debates per position. They would also organize the debates themselves, a deviation from 2016 where they let the media organizations organize it.<sup about="#mwt721" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-181"}}' id="cite_ref-181" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-181" id="mwBZ0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBZ4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBZ8">[</span>178<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBaA">]</span></span></a></sup> By January 2022, the commission said they might forego with the teaser debate, as the number of candidates had been reduced to a more manageable number.<sup about="#mwt724" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-182"}}' id="cite_ref-182" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-182" id="mwBaE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBaI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBaM">[</span>179<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBaQ">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBaU">The commission, by law, could not compel candidates to join debates, but expected them to do so as it would present them with massive exposure, and that it would not count towards the limits of the airtime that they were allowed to advertise on broadcast networks.<sup about="#mwt727" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-183"}}' id="cite_ref-183" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-183" id="mwBaY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBac"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBag">[</span>180<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBak">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBao">The presidential debate organized by <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sonshine_Media_Network_International" id="mwBas" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sonshine Media Network International">Sonshine Media Network International</a> (SMNI) was noted for the absences of several candidates. Manny Pacquiao declined to participate due to the indictment of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apollo_Quiboloy" id="mwBaw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Apollo Quiboloy">Apollo Quiboloy</a>, leader of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kingdom_of_Jesus_Christ_(church)" id="mwBa0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Kingdom of Jesus Christ (church)">Kingdom of Jesus Christ</a> which is affiliated with SMNI, allegedly for sex trafficking by the United States federal government. Panfilo Lacson on his part declined due to Quiboloy having already openly endorsed the candidacy of Bongbong Marcos while Leni Robredo and Isko Moreno cited scheduling conflicts and prior commitments for their non-attendance.<sup about="#mwt730" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-184"}}' id="cite_ref-184" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-184" id="mwBa4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBa8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBbA">[</span>181<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBbE">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBbI">This came at the heels of Marcos declining an invitation of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CNN_Philippines" id="mwBbM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="CNN Philippines">CNN Philippines</a> for their presidential debate, citing prior commitments.<sup about="#mwt733" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-185"}}' id="cite_ref-185" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-185" id="mwBbQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBbU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBbY">[</span>182<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBbc">]</span></span></a></sup> Marcos had earlier declined to join the forum organized by the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kapisanan_ng_mga_Brodkaster_ng_Pilipinas" id="mwBbg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Kapisanan ng mga Brodkaster ng Pilipinas">Kapisanan ng mga Brodkaster ng Pilipinas</a> (the broadcasters association) for the same reason.<sup about="#mwt736" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-186"}}' id="cite_ref-186" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-186" id="mwBbk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBbo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBbs">[</span>183<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBbw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBb0">SMNI had also planned to hold a vice-presidential debate on February 22, 2022. The debate was later cancelled to make way for a second presidential debate.<sup about="#mwt739" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":25"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:25-187"}}' id="cite_ref-:25_187-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:25-187" id="mwBb4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBb8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBcA">[</span>184<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBcE">]</span></span></a></sup> Prior to its cancellation, two candidates, Sotto and Pangilinan, had declined to participate.<sup about="#mwt742" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Talabong1922"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Talabong1922-188"}}' id="cite_ref-Talabong1922_188-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Talabong1922-188" id="mwBcI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBcM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBcQ">[</span>185<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBcU">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<table id="mwBcY" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; text-align:center;" width="75%">

<td><span class="nowrap">Mar 19</span></td>
<td rowspan="3"><a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COMELEC" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="COMELEC">COMELEC</a></td>
<td rowspan="2"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Broadcast_syndication" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Broadcast syndication">Syndication</a></td><td rowspan="3"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sofitel_Philippine_Plaza_Manila" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sofitel Philippine Plaza Manila">Sofitel Philippine Plaza Manila</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pasay" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pasay">Pasay</a></td><td><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luchi_Cruz-Valdes" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Luchi Cruz-Valdes">Luchi Cruz-Valdez</a></td>

<td style="text-align:center;"><sup about="#mwt752" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-189"}}' id="cite_ref-189" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-189" id="mwBdQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBdU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBdY">[</span>186<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBdc">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt757" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-190"}}' id="cite_ref-190" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-190" id="mwBdg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBdk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBdo">[</span>187<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBds">]</span></span></a></sup></td></tr>
<tr>

</section><section aria-labelledby="Opinion_polling" data-mw-section-id="18" id="mwBgQ"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Opinion_polling">Opinion polling</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=18" title="Edit section: Opinion polling"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt805" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Opinion polling for the 2022 Philippine presidential election"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBgU" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt806" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt805" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwBgY" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Opinion_polling_for_the_2022_Philippine_presidential_election" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Opinion polling for the 2022 Philippine presidential election">Opinion polling for the 2022 Philippine presidential election</a></div>
<p id="mwBgc"><a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Opinion_polling" id="mwBgg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Opinion polling">Opinion polling</a>, commonly known as "surveys" in the Philippines, is conducted by <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Social_Weather_Stations" id="mwBgk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Social Weather Stations">Social Weather Stations</a> (SWS), <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pulse_Asia" id="mwBgo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pulse Asia">Pulse Asia</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OCTA_Research" id="mwBgs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="OCTA Research">OCTA Research</a>, and other third-party pollsters.</p><p id="mwBgw">

The tables below the latest six polls that were administered.</p><span about="#mwt807" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"wide image","href":"./Template:Wide_image"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Opinion polls for Philippine presidential election 2022.svg"},"2":{"wt":"950px"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwBg0" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><style about="#mwt808" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Wide image/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' data-mw-deduplicate="TemplateStyles:r1330227556" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles">.mw-parser-output .wide-image-normbg,.mw-parser-output .thumbimage.wide-image-normbg{background:var(--background-color-neutral-subtle,#f8f9fa);color:inherit}.mw-parser-output .wide-image-nobg,.mw-parser-output .thumbimage.wide-image-nobg{background:transparent;color:inherit}</style></span><div about="#mwt807" class="thumb tnone" id="mwBg4" style="margin-left:auto;margin-right:auto;overflow:hidden;width:auto;max-width:960px"><div class="thumbinner"><div class="noresize thumbimage" style="overflow:auto"><span data-mw='{"caption":""}' typeof="mw:File"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Opinion_polls_for_Philippine_presidential_election_2022.svg"><img alt="" class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="228" data-file-type="drawing" data-file-width="512" decoding="async" height="423" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Opinion_polls_for_Philippine_presidential_election_2022.svg" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/78/Opinion_polls_for_Philippine_presidential_election_2022.svg/960px-Opinion_polls_for_Philippine_presidential_election_2022.svg.png" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/78/Opinion_polls_for_Philippine_presidential_election_2022.svg/1920px-Opinion_polls_for_Philippine_presidential_election_2022.svg.png 2x" width="950"/></a></span></div></div></div>
<span about="#mwt809" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"#section:Opinion polling for the 2022 Philippine presidential election","function":"lst"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Presidential surveys table"}},"i":0}},"\n",{"template":{"target":{"wt":"s-end","href":"./Template:S-end"},"params":{},"i":1}}]}' id="mwBg8" typeof="mw:Transclusion"></span><table about="#mwt809" class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;font-size:85%;line-height:14px;">
<tbody><tr><th rowspan="2">Fieldwork<br/>date(s)</th>
<th rowspan="2">Pollster</th>
<th data-sort-type="number" rowspan="2">Sample<br/>size</th>
<th data-sort-type="number" rowspan="2"><abbr title="Margin of error">MoE</abbr></th>
<th class="unsortable" style="width:60px;"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ernesto_Abella" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ernesto Abella">Abella</a><br/><small><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Independent_politician" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Independent politician">Ind.</a></small></th>
<th class="unsortable" style="width:60px;"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leody_de_Guzman" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leody de Guzman">De Guzman</a><br/><small><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Partido_Lakas_ng_Masa" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Partido Lakas ng Masa">PLM</a></small></th>
<th class="unsortable" style="width:60px;"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Norberto_Gonzales" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Norberto Gonzales">Gonzales</a><br/><small><a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philippine_Democratic_Socialist_Party" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Philippine Democratic Socialist Party">PDSP</a></small></th>
