</section><section aria-labelledby="Campaign" data-mw-section-id="19" id="mwBjY"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Campaign">Campaign</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=19" title="Edit section: Campaign"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<figure class="mw-default-size" id="mwBjc" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Vote-rich_provinces_in_the_2022_Philippine_elections.png" id="mwBjg"><img class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="3120" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="2000" decoding="async" height="390" id="mwBjk" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Vote-rich_provinces_in_the_2022_Philippine_elections.png" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1c/Vote-rich_provinces_in_the_2022_Philippine_elections.png/250px-Vote-rich_provinces_in_the_2022_Philippine_elections.png" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1c/Vote-rich_provinces_in_the_2022_Philippine_elections.png/500px-Vote-rich_provinces_in_the_2022_Philippine_elections.png 2x" width="250"/></a><figcaption id="mwBjo">"Vote-rich provinces," or provinces and Metro Manila as a whole that have more than one million voters. Nationally elected candidates and parties typically campaign in these areas to reduce costs.</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mwBjs">Candidates began their campaign-related activities such as <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Motorcade" id="mwBjw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Motorcade">motorcades</a>, <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Campaign_sortie" id="mwBj0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Campaign sortie">gatherings</a> and caravans as early as late 2021, even before the mandated start of the campaign period by the COMELEC.<sup about="#mwt857" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-209"}}' id="cite_ref-209" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-209" id="mwBj4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBj8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBkA">[</span>205<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBkE">]</span></span></a></sup></p>

<td id="mwBsI">Pink and green<sup about="#mwt890" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-219"}}' id="cite_ref-219" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-219" id="mwBsM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBsQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBsU">[</span>215<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBsY">]</span></span></a></sup></td>
<td id="mwBsc"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bam_Aquino" id="mwBsg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bam Aquino">Bam Aquino</a><sup about="#mwt893" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-220"}}' id="cite_ref-220" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-220" id="mwBsk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBso"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBss">[</span>216<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBsw">]</span></span></a></sup></td>
<td id="mwBs0">"<i id="mwBs4">Gobyernong Tapat, Angat Buhay Lahat</i>"</td>
<td id="mwBs8">"[With an] honest government, is a better life for all"<sup about="#mwt896" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-221"}}' id="cite_ref-221" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-221" id="mwBtA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBtE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBtI">[</span>217<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBtM">]</span></span></a></sup></td>
<td id="mwBtQ"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leni_Robredo_2022_presidential_campaign" id="mwBtU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leni Robredo 2022 presidential campaign">Details</a></td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<section aria-labelledby="Start_of_campaign_period_for_national_positions" data-mw-section-id="20" id="mwBtY"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Start_of_campaign_period_for_national_positions">Start of campaign period for national positions</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=20" title="Edit section: Start of campaign period for national positions"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<ul about="#mwt897" class="gallery mw-gallery-traditional" data-mw='{"name":"gallery","attrs":{"widths":"180"},"body":{}}' id="mwBtc" typeof="mw:Extension/gallery">
<li class="gallerybox" id="mwBtg" style="width: 215px;"><div class="thumb" id="mwBtk" style="width: 210px; height: 150px;"><span id="mwBto" typeof="mw:File"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:BBM_motorcade_Makati,_Bongbong_Marcos_Estrella_(J.P._Rizal,_Makati;_02-13-2022).jpg" id="mwBts" title="Bongbong Marcos campaigning in Makati"><img alt="Bongbong Marcos campaigning in Makati" class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="2664" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="3552" decoding="async" height="120" id="mwBtw" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:BBM_motorcade_Makati,_Bongbong_Marcos_Estrella_(J.P._Rizal,_Makati;_02-13-2022).jpg" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/87/BBM_motorcade_Makati%2C_Bongbong_Marcos_Estrella_%28J.P._Rizal%2C_Makati%3B_02-13-2022%29.jpg/250px-BBM_motorcade_Makati%2C_Bongbong_Marcos_Estrella_%28J.P._Rizal%2C_Makati%3B_02-13-2022%29.jpg" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/87/BBM_motorcade_Makati%2C_Bongbong_Marcos_Estrella_%28J.P._Rizal%2C_Makati%3B_02-13-2022%29.jpg/330px-BBM_motorcade_Makati%2C_Bongbong_Marcos_Estrella_%28J.P._Rizal%2C_Makati%3B_02-13-2022%29.jpg 2x" width="160"/></a></span></div><div class="gallerytext" id="mwBt0">Bongbong Marcos campaigning in Makati</div></li>
<li class="gallerybox" id="mwBt4" style="width: 215px;"><div class="thumb" id="mwBt8" style="width: 210px; height: 150px;"><span id="mwBuA" typeof="mw:File"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Isko_Moreno_campaign,_Navotas_(Navotas;_02-11-2022)_6.jpg" id="mwBuE" title="Isko Moreno and Willie Ong campaigning in Navotas"><img alt="Isko Moreno and Willie Ong campaigning in Navotas" class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="2664" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="3552" decoding="async" height="120" id="mwBuI" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Isko_Moreno_campaign,_Navotas_(Navotas;_02-11-2022)_6.jpg" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b0/Isko_Moreno_campaign%2C_Navotas_%28Navotas%3B_02-11-2022%29_6.jpg/250px-Isko_Moreno_campaign%2C_Navotas_%28Navotas%3B_02-11-2022%29_6.jpg" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b0/Isko_Moreno_campaign%2C_Navotas_%28Navotas%3B_02-11-2022%29_6.jpg/330px-Isko_Moreno_campaign%2C_Navotas_%28Navotas%3B_02-11-2022%29_6.jpg 2x" width="160"/></a></span></div><div class="gallerytext" id="mwBuM">Isko Moreno and Willie Ong campaigning in Navotas</div></li>
<li class="gallerybox" id="mwBuQ" style="width: 215px;"><div class="thumb" id="mwBuU" style="width: 210px; height: 150px;"><span id="mwBuY" typeof="mw:File"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Leni_for_QC_rally,_Robredo-Pangilinan_kaway_liwasan_(QMC,_Quezon_City;_02-13-2022)_1.jpg" id="mwBuc" title="Leni Robredo and Francis Pangilinan campaigning at the Quezon Memorial Circle"><img alt="Leni Robredo and Francis Pangilinan campaigning at the Quezon Memorial Circle" class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="2664" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="3552" decoding="async" height="120" id="mwBug" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Leni_for_QC_rally,_Robredo-Pangilinan_kaway_liwasan_(QMC,_Quezon_City;_02-13-2022)_1.jpg" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/64/Leni_for_QC_rally%2C_Robredo-Pangilinan_kaway_liwasan_%28QMC%2C_Quezon_City%3B_02-13-2022%29_1.jpg/250px-Leni_for_QC_rally%2C_Robredo-Pangilinan_kaway_liwasan_%28QMC%2C_Quezon_City%3B_02-13-2022%29_1.jpg" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/64/Leni_for_QC_rally%2C_Robredo-Pangilinan_kaway_liwasan_%28QMC%2C_Quezon_City%3B_02-13-2022%29_1.jpg/330px-Leni_for_QC_rally%2C_Robredo-Pangilinan_kaway_liwasan_%28QMC%2C_Quezon_City%3B_02-13-2022%29_1.jpg 2x" width="160"/></a></span></div><div class="gallerytext" id="mwBuk">Leni Robredo and Francis Pangilinan campaigning at the Quezon Memorial Circle</div></li>
<li class="gallerybox" id="mwBuo" style="width: 215px;"><div class="thumb" id="mwBus" style="width: 210px; height: 150px;"><span id="mwBuw" typeof="mw:File"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Manny_Pacquiao_election_motorcade_Marikina,_Molina_(Marikina;_02-28-2022).jpg" id="mwBu0" title="Manny Pacquiao campaigning in Marikina"><img alt="Manny Pacquiao campaigning in Marikina" class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="2664" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="3552" decoding="async" height="120" id="mwBu4" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Manny_Pacquiao_election_motorcade_Marikina,_Molina_(Marikina;_02-28-2022).jpg" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/55/Manny_Pacquiao_election_motorcade_Marikina%2C_Molina_%28Marikina%3B_02-28-2022%29.jpg/250px-Manny_Pacquiao_election_motorcade_Marikina%2C_Molina_%28Marikina%3B_02-28-2022%29.jpg" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/55/Manny_Pacquiao_election_motorcade_Marikina%2C_Molina_%28Marikina%3B_02-28-2022%29.jpg/330px-Manny_Pacquiao_election_motorcade_Marikina%2C_Molina_%28Marikina%3B_02-28-2022%29.jpg 2x" width="160"/></a></span></div><div class="gallerytext" id="mwBu8">Manny Pacquiao campaigning in Marikina </div></li>
<li class="gallerybox" id="mwBvA" style="width: 215px;"><div class="thumb" id="mwBvE" style="width: 210px; height: 150px;"><span id="mwBvI" typeof="mw:File"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Lacson-Sotto_rally_Pasig_Megamarket_Ping_forum_layo_(Pasig;_03-09-2022).jpg" id="mwBvM" title="Panfilo Lacson and Tito Sotto at a town hall meeting in Pasig"><img alt="Panfilo Lacson and Tito Sotto at a town hall meeting in Pasig" class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="2664" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="3552" decoding="async" height="120" id="mwBvQ" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Lacson-Sotto_rally_Pasig_Megamarket_Ping_forum_layo_(Pasig;_03-09-2022).jpg" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/64/Lacson-Sotto_rally_Pasig_Megamarket_Ping_forum_layo_%28Pasig%3B_03-09-2022%29.jpg/250px-Lacson-Sotto_rally_Pasig_Megamarket_Ping_forum_layo_%28Pasig%3B_03-09-2022%29.jpg" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/64/Lacson-Sotto_rally_Pasig_Megamarket_Ping_forum_layo_%28Pasig%3B_03-09-2022%29.jpg/330px-Lacson-Sotto_rally_Pasig_Megamarket_Ping_forum_layo_%28Pasig%3B_03-09-2022%29.jpg 2x" width="160"/></a></span></div><div class="gallerytext" id="mwBvU">Panfilo Lacson and Tito Sotto at a town hall meeting in Pasig</div></li>
</ul>
<p id="mwBvY">Presidential tickets held their proclamation rallies on February 8, 2022, the start of the campaign period for national positions.<sup about="#mwt898" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":0"}}' id="cite_ref-:0_198-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:0-198" id="mwBvc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBvg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBvk">[</span>195<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBvo">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt901" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-222"}}' id="cite_ref-222" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-222" id="mwBvs"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBvw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBv0">[</span>218<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBv4">]</span></span></a></sup> The ticket of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Faisal_Mangondato" id="mwBv8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Faisal Mangondato">Faisal Mangondato</a> and Carlos Serapio had already held their prayer proclamation during the previous weekend in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baguio" id="mwBwA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Baguio">Baguio</a>.<sup about="#mwt904" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-223"}}' id="cite_ref-223" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-223" id="mwBwE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBwI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBwM">[</span>219<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBwQ">]</span></span></a></sup> The Marcos and Duterte tandem started their campaign at the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philippine_Arena" id="mwBwU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Philippine Arena">Philippine Arena</a> in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bulacan" id="mwBwY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bulacan">Bulacan</a>.<sup about="#mwt907" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-224"}}' id="cite_ref-224" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-224" id="mwBwc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBwg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBwk">[</span>220<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBwo">]</span></span></a></sup> Robredo and Pangilinan held their rally in Plaza Quezon, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Naga,_Camarines_Sur" id="mwBws" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Naga, Camarines Sur">Naga</a>, Robredo's hometown, on February 8.<sup about="#mwt910" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-225"}}' id="cite_ref-225" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-225" id="mwBww"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBw0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBw4">[</span>221<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBw8">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBxA">Isko Moreno and Willie Ong kicked off their campaign at the <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kartilya_ng_Katipunan_Monument" id="mwBxE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Kartilya ng Katipunan Monument">Kartilya ng Katipunan</a> just outside <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manila_City_Hall" id="mwBxI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Manila City Hall">Manila City Hall</a>. The Lacson and Sotto tandem held their proclamation rally at the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/City_of_Imus_Grandstand_and_Track_Oval" id="mwBxM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="City of Imus Grandstand and Track Oval">Imus Grandstand</a> in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Imus" id="mwBxQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Imus">Imus</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cavite" id="mwBxU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cavite">Cavite</a>, Lacson's hometown. Pacquiao and Atienza commenced their campaign at the Oval Plaza in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/General_Santos" id="mwBxY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="General Santos">General Santos</a>, where Pacquiao grew up. The de Guzman and Bello tandem launched their campaign at the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bantayog_ng_mga_Bayani" id="mwBxc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bantayog ng mga Bayani">Bantayog ng mga Bayani</a> in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quezon_City" id="mwBxg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Quezon City">Quezon City</a>.<sup about="#mwt913" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":0"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:0-198"}}' id="cite_ref-:0_198-2" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:0-198" id="mwBxk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBxo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBxs">[</span>195<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBxw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBx0">Ernesto Abella had his proclamation rally in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dasmariñas" id="mwBx4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Dasmariñas">Dasmariñas</a>, Cavite,<sup about="#mwt916" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-226"}}' id="cite_ref-226" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-226" id="mwBx8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwByA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwByE">[</span>222<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwByI">]</span></span></a></sup> while the Montemayor and David ticket started their campaign in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pasay" id="mwByM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pasay">Pasay</a>.<sup about="#mwt917" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":6"}}' id="cite_ref-:6_215-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:6-215" id="mwByQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwByU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwByY">[</span>211<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwByc">]</span></span></a></sup> President Duterte, on his <i id="mwByg">Talk to the People</i> TV show, notably did not endorse a candidate going into the campaign period, saying that "at this time, I am saying that I am not supporting anybody."<sup about="#mwt920" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-227"}}' id="cite_ref-227" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-227" id="mwByk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwByo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBys">[</span>223<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwByw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBy0">While most tickets had their proclamation rallies in the respective hometowns of the presidential candidate, the Marcos campaign explained that they had chosen the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philippine_Arena" id="mwBy4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Philippine Arena">Philippine Arena</a> to seat their thousands of supporters, for which 25,000 tickets were made available.<sup about="#mwt921" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":112"}}' id="cite_ref-:112_157-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:112-157" id="mwBy8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBzA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBzE">[</span>154<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBzI">]</span></span></a></sup> Meanwhile, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lito_Atienza" id="mwBzM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Lito Atienza">Lito Atienza</a> failed to make it to General Santos as he had suffered an injury prior to the event.<sup about="#mwt924" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-228"}}' id="cite_ref-228" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-228" id="mwBzQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBzU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBzY">[</span>224<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBzc">]</span></span></a></sup> Prior to their rally, the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Partido_Lakas_ng_Masa" id="mwBzg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Partido Lakas ng Masa">Partido Lakas ng Masa</a> failed to secure a permit from the Commission on Elections to hold it at the Bantayog ng mga Bayani. De Guzman said the permit had been refused because required documents had not been submitted. A rally without a permit is grounds for disqualifying a candidate.<sup about="#mwt927" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-229"}}' id="cite_ref-229" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-229" id="mwBzk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwBzo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBzs">[</span>225<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwBzw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwBz0">Aside from the listed presidential tickets, other tandems were pushed. Representative <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joey_Salceda" id="mwBz4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Joey Salceda">Joey Salceda</a> pushed for a "Leni–Sara" (ROSA) tandem, endorsing Robredo in their campaign in Albay, while endorsing Duterte the next day.<sup about="#mwt930" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-230"}}' id="cite_ref-230" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-230" id="mwBz8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB0A"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB0E">[</span>226<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB0I">]</span></span></a></sup> In Mindanao, an "Isko–Sara" (ISSA) tandem was promoted when Moreno had a campaign rally in there, where his running mate Willie Ong skipped. The Moreno campaign defended Ong's absence, saying that the ISSA tarpaulins would have brought him into an awkward situation.<sup about="#mwt933" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-231"}}' id="cite_ref-231" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-231" id="mwB0M"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB0Q"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB0U">[</span>227<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB0Y">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwB0c">By mid-March, the <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bureau_of_Internal_Revenue_(Philippines)" id="mwB0g" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bureau of Internal Revenue (Philippines)">Bureau of Internal Revenue</a> (BIR) confirmed reports that <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aksyon_Demokratiko" id="mwB0k" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Aksyon Demokratiko">Aksyon Demokratiko</a>, Moreno's political party, had asked if they had ordered the estate of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ferdinand_Marcos" id="mwB0o" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ferdinand Marcos">Ferdinand Marcos</a>, Bongbong's father and former President, to pay the ₱203 billion-peso (US$3.8 billion) tax liabilities.<sup about="#mwt936" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-232"}}' id="cite_ref-232" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-232" id="mwB0s"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB0w"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB00">[</span>228<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB04">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt939" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-233"}}' id="cite_ref-233" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-233" id="mwB08"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB1A"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB1E">[</span>229<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB1I">]</span></span></a></sup> Marcos's camp had earlier said that the case was under litigation, with the younger Marcos himself stating that "There's a lot of fake news involved there." The <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Presidential_Commission_on_Good_Government" id="mwB1M" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Presidential Commission on Good Government">Presidential Commission on Good Government</a>, the agency tasked with recovering the ill-gotten wealth of the Marcoses, denied that the case was under litigation, saying that the judgment was "as early as 1997, the judgment on the tax case had become final and executory."<sup about="#mwt942" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Mercado"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Mercado-234"}}' id="cite_ref-Mercado_234-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Mercado-234" id="mwB1Q"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB1U"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB1Y">[</span>230<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB1c">]</span></span></a></sup> In the first <i id="mwB1g"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PiliPinas_Debates_2022" id="mwB1k" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="PiliPinas Debates 2022">PiliPinas Debates 2022</a></i>, Moreno, Robredo, Lacson and de Guzman called on the heirs of the elder Marcos, including the younger Marcos who did not attend the debate, to pay the estate and income taxes owed to the state.<sup about="#mwt943" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Mercado"}}' id="cite_ref-Mercado_234-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Mercado-234" id="mwB1o"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB1s"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB1w">[</span>230<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB10">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwB14">On March 24, 2022, amid reports of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Partido_para_sa_Demokratikong_Reporma" id="mwB18" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Partido para sa Demokratikong Reporma">Partido Reporma</a> and its key officials rescinding their endorsement of Lacson and endorsing another candidate, Lacson resigned as Partido Reporma chairman<sup about="#mwt946" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-235"}}' id="cite_ref-235" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-235" id="mwB2A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB2E"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB2I">[</span>231<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB2M">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt949" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-236"}}' id="cite_ref-236" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-236" id="mwB2Q"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB2U"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB2Y">[</span>232<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB2c">]</span></span></a></sup> and as party's standard bearer, making him an independent candidate,<sup about="#mwt952" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-237"}}' id="cite_ref-237" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-237" id="mwB2g"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB2k"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB2o">[</span>233<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB2s">]</span></span></a></sup> although Reporma would  still be listed as his party in the ballots and would be used to determine the dominant party if he won.<sup about="#mwt955" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-238"}}' id="cite_ref-238" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-238" id="mwB2w"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB20"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB24">[</span>234<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB28">]</span></span></a></sup> The party's president, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pantaleon_Alvarez" id="mwB3A" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pantaleon Alvarez">Pantaleon Alvarez</a>, later announced that they would be switching their endorsement to Robredo for president.<sup about="#mwt958" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-239"}}' id="cite_ref-239" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-239" id="mwB3E"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB3I"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB3M">[</span>235<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB3Q">]</span></span></a></sup> However, some Reporma members, including founder and chairman emeritus, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Renato_de_Villa" id="mwB3U" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Renato de Villa">Renato de Villa</a>, maintained their support for Lacson.<sup about="#mwt961" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":1c"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:1c-240"}}' id="cite_ref-:1c_240-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:1c-240" id="mwB3Y"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB3c"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB3g">[</span>236<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB3k">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt964" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-241"}}' id="cite_ref-241" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-241" id="mwB3o"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB3s"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB3w">[</span>237<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB30">]</span></span></a></sup> Sources inside the party stated that Lacson already knew about the switch weeks earlier, but was permitted to resign as a way to save face.<sup about="#mwt967" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-242"}}' id="cite_ref-242" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-242" id="mwB34"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB38"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB4A">[</span>238<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB4E">]</span></span></a></sup> Lacson later claimed that Alvarez' withdrawal of support stemmed from his inability to provide ₱800 million in additional funding for Reporma's local candidates, which Alvarez denied.<sup about="#mwt970" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-243"}}' id="cite_ref-243" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-243" id="mwB4I"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB4M"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB4Q">[</span>239<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB4U">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt971" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":1c"}}' id="cite_ref-:1c_240-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:1c-240" id="mwB4Y"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB4c"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB4g">[</span>236<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB4k">]</span></span></a></sup> Sotto remained as Reporma's vice presidential candidate.<sup about="#mwt974" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-244"}}' id="cite_ref-244" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-244" id="mwB4o"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB4s"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB4w">[</span>240<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB40">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Start_of_campaign_period_for_local_positions" data-mw-section-id="21" id="mwB44"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Start_of_campaign_period_for_local_positions">Start of campaign period for local positions</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=21" title="Edit section: Start of campaign period for local positions"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwB48">On March 31, Lito Atienza, who had not been able to campaign after suffering an accident, said that he was seriously considering withdrawing in order to broker a Pacquiao–Sotto tandem to defeat the Marcos–Duterte ticket. To this end, he urged Panfilo Lacson to withdraw and promised to follow suit.<sup about="#mwt977" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-245"}}' id="cite_ref-245" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-245" id="mwB5A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB5E"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB5I">[</span>241<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB5M">]</span></span></a></sup> Lacson rebuffed the offer, saying that Atienza should learn some manners.<sup about="#mwt980" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-246"}}' id="cite_ref-246" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-246" id="mwB5Q"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB5U"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB5Y">[</span>242<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB5c">]</span></span></a></sup> Atienza said he felt insulted by Lacson's reply to his suggestion.<sup about="#mwt983" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-247"}}' id="cite_ref-247" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-247" id="mwB5g"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB5k"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB5o">[</span>243<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB5s">]</span></span></a></sup> A week later, Atienza publicly apologized to Lacson, saying he had not anticipated Lacson's reaction to his suggestion,<sup about="#mwt986" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-248"}}' id="cite_ref-248" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-248" id="mwB5w"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB50"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB54">[</span>244<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB58">]</span></span></a></sup> and Lacson accepted Atienza's apology.<sup about="#mwt989" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-249"}}' id="cite_ref-249" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-249" id="mwB6A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB6E"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB6I">[</span>245<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB6M">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwB6Q">On April 9, Lacson revealed that the camp of vice president Robredo had asked him to withdraw in favor of a Robredo–Sotto tandem.<sup about="#mwt992" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-250"}}' id="cite_ref-250" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-250" id="mwB6U"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB6Y"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB6c">[</span>246<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB6g">]</span></span></a></sup> He also claimed that Moreno and Pacquiao were given similar requests to withdraw in favor of Robredo. He later revealed the person who had asked him to withdraw was former <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mayor_of_Quezon_City" id="mwB6k" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mayor of Quezon City">Quezon City mayor</a> Brigido "Jun" Simon Jr.<sup about="#mwt995" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-251"}}' id="cite_ref-251" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-251" id="mwB6o"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB6s"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB6w">[</span>247<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB60">]</span></span></a></sup> Robredo's camp denied the claims, stating that no moves were made by their camp and that Simon was unauthorized to represent her campaign.<sup about="#mwt998" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-252"}}' id="cite_ref-252" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-252" id="mwB64"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB68"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB7A">[</span>248<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB7E">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt1001" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-253"}}' id="cite_ref-253" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-253" id="mwB7I"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB7M"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB7Q">[</span>249<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB7U">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<figure class="mw-default-size" id="mwB7Y" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:ManilaPeninsulaEasterSundayJointPressCon.jpg" id="mwB7c"><img class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="1414" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="2255" decoding="async" height="157" id="mwB7g" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:ManilaPeninsulaEasterSundayJointPressCon.jpg" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/73/ManilaPeninsulaEasterSundayJointPressCon.jpg/250px-ManilaPeninsulaEasterSundayJointPressCon.jpg" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/73/ManilaPeninsulaEasterSundayJointPressCon.jpg/500px-ManilaPeninsulaEasterSundayJointPressCon.jpg 2x" width="250"/></a><figcaption id="mwB7k">From left to right: Presidential candidates <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isko_Moreno" id="mwB7o" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Isko Moreno">Isko Moreno</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Norberto_Gonzales" id="mwB7s" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Norberto Gonzales">Norberto Gonzales</a>, and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panfilo_Lacson" id="mwB7w" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Panfilo Lacson">Panfilo Lacson</a> hold a joint press conference on fighting election sabotage during a press conference at <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Peninsula_Manila" id="mwB70" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="The Peninsula Manila">The Peninsula Manila</a> on April 17, 2022. Not present in the event is <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manny_Pacquiao" id="mwB74" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Manny Pacquiao">Manny Pacquiao</a> who also signed the joint statement.</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mwB78">On April 17, three presidential candidates, Gonzales, Lacson, and Moreno, held a press conference affirming that they would not withdraw their candidacies despite several calls for them to back down in favor of another candidate. Abella was initially reported to attend but did not show up while Pacquiao, who committed to catch up, was not able to arrive before it ended due to commitments in General Santos.<sup about="#mwt1004" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-254"}}' id="cite_ref-254" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-254" id="mwB8A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB8E"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB8I">[</span>250<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB8M">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt1007" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-255"}}' id="cite_ref-255" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-255" id="mwB8Q"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB8U"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB8Y">[</span>251<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB8c">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt1010" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-256"}}' id="cite_ref-256" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-256" id="mwB8g"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB8k"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB8o">[</span>252<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB8s">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt1013" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-257"}}' id="cite_ref-257" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-257" id="mwB8w"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB80"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB84">[</span>253<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB88">]</span></span></a></sup> The three candidates, along with Pacquiao, signed a joint statement declaring their intention to continue their candidacies. According to Lacson, they held the press conference to remind voters that the election was not a two-way race between Marcos and Robredo.<sup about="#mwt1016" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-258"}}' id="cite_ref-258" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-258" id="mwB9A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB9E"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB9I">[</span>254<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB9M">]</span></span></a></sup> They criticized an unnamed group for allegedly offering money in exchange for their withdrawal and for stripping them of their support groups, although Moreno mentioned the "pink" and "yellow" as perpetrators; both colors were associated with the Robredo campaign.<sup about="#mwt1019" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-259"}}' id="cite_ref-259" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-259" id="mwB9Q"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB9U"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB9Y">[</span>255<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB9c">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwB9g">Moreno chastised Robredo for breaking her promise not to run and called her untrustworthy after purportedly "fooling" them during unification talks.<sup about="#mwt1022" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-260"}}' id="cite_ref-260" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-260" id="mwB9k"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB9o"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB9s">[</span>256<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB9w">]</span></span></a></sup> Lacson also questioned Robredo's character after some of her campaign staff were allegedly amenable to dropping her running mate, Pangilinan, in exchange for more votes in some provinces. Moreno later called for her to withdraw instead, asserting that Robredo only ran to defeat Marcos, not for the country, and that being the top second choice of Marcos voters, he had much bigger chance to defeat him since he does not have the "<i id="mwB90"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liberal_Party_(Philippines)" id="mwB94" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Liberal Party (Philippines)">dilawan</a></i>" baggage, claiming that not all who will vote for Marcos are loyalists, but would rather vote for Marcos instead to ensure that someone associated with the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liberal_Party_(Philippines)" id="mwB98" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Liberal Party (Philippines)">Liberal Party</a> will not be elected president anymore.<sup about="#mwt1025" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-261"}}' id="cite_ref-261" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-261" id="mwB-A"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB-E"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB-I">[</span>257<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB-M">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt1028" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-262"}}' id="cite_ref-262" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-262" id="mwB-Q"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB-U"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB-Y">[</span>258<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB-c">]</span></span></a></sup> Robredo's camp later released a statement questioning the purpose of the press conference, calling the event unnecessary "theatrics," and rejected Moreno's call for her to withdraw from the race. Her camp also stated that they were thankful "that the alignments have been made even clearer."<sup about="#mwt1031" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-263"}}' id="cite_ref-263" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-263" id="mwB-g"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB-k"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB-o">[</span>259<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB-s">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwB-w">Hours following the press conference, old photos of Moreno and Marcos dining together resurfaced.<sup about="#mwt1034" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-264"}}' id="cite_ref-264" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-264" id="mwB-0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB-4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB-8">[</span>260<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB_A">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt1037" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-265"}}' id="cite_ref-265" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-265" id="mwB_E"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB_I"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB_M">[</span>261<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB_Q">]</span></span></a></sup> The photo, taken in February 2021 during the reopening of a restaurant in Manila, attracted allegations from Robredo's supporters that Moreno has been meeting with Marcos and secretly working for him to undermine Robredo and her campaign. Moreno denied the allegations, reiterated that it was their camp who first brought up the issue on the Marcos family's unpaid ₱203 billion estate tax, and criticized Robredo's camp for "playing dirty".<sup about="#mwt1040" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-266"}}' id="cite_ref-266" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-266" id="mwB_U"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB_Y"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB_c">[</span>262<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB_g">]</span></span></a></sup> Moreno later said that after months of not commenting, he only retaliated after supporters of Robredo asked him to withdraw since October 2021 when Robredo announced her candidacy, describing it as a "fair call" and a "taste of their own medicine".<sup about="#mwt1043" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-267"}}' id="cite_ref-267" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-267" id="mwB_k"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB_o"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB_s">[</span>263<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwB_w">]</span></span></a></sup> After being accused of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Toxic_masculinity" id="mwB_0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Toxic masculinity">toxic masculinity</a> by Robredo supporters, Moreno also defended his criticism against Robredo as a "character issue" and has nothing to do with gender.<sup about="#mwt1046" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-268"}}' id="cite_ref-268" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-268" id="mwB_4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwB_8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCAA">[</span>264<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCAE">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwCAI">DPP's Rizalito David, who had earlier endorsed his rival vice presidential candidate Sotto for that position, endorsed in COMELEC's <i id="mwCAM"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PiliPinas_Forum_2022" id="mwCAQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="PiliPinas Forum 2022">PiliPinas Forum 2022</a></i>, in front of his presidential running mate Jose Montemayor, Robredo for president, calling frontrunners Marcos and Duterte as "clear and present danger" to the country. David himself did not withdraw from the election, however.<sup about="#mwt1049" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-269"}}' id="cite_ref-269" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-269" id="mwCAU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCAY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCAc">[</span>265<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCAg">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Miting_de_avance" data-mw-section-id="22" id="mwCAk"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Miting_de_avance">Miting de avance</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=22" title="Edit section: Miting de avance"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<table align="right" id="mwCAo">
<tbody id="mwCAs"><tr id="mwCAw">

rel="mw:WikiLink" title="File:Philippines location map (square).svg"> </a></div>Miting de avances outside Metro Manila.</div></div></div></td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<p id="mwCBM">The "miting de avance" is the final political rally of the candidates, usually held on the last day of the campaign period or two days before election day. Some candidates opted to hold multiple mitings de avance.<sup about="#mwt1056" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-270"}}' id="cite_ref-270" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-270" id="mwCBQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCBU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCBY">[</span>266<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCBc">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwCBg">Marcos's miting de avance was held in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bay_City,_Metro_Manila" id="mwCBk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bay City, Metro Manila">Bay City</a> in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pasay" id="mwCBo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pasay">Pasay</a> on May 7.<sup about="#mwt1059" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-271"}}' id="cite_ref-271" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-271" id="mwCBs"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCBw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCB0">[</span>267<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCB4">]</span></span></a></sup> Prior to that, he held two mitings de avance, one in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guimbal" id="mwCB8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Guimbal">Guimbal</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iloilo" id="mwCCA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Iloilo">Iloilo</a> on May 3, and another on May 5 in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tagum" id="mwCCE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Tagum">Tagum</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_del_Norte" id="mwCCI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao del Norte">Davao del Norte</a>. In total, the Marcos campaign held three mitings de avance, one for each <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Island_groups_of_the_Philippines" id="mwCCM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Island groups of the Philippines">island group of the country</a>. Meanwhile, Robredo capped her campaign at her miting de avance in <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ayala_Triangle" id="mwCCQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ayala Triangle">Ayala Triangle</a> in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Makati" id="mwCCU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Makati">Makati</a> on May 7, preceded by an earlier miting de avance at Magsaysay Avenue in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Naga,_Camarines_Sur" id="mwCCY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Naga, Camarines Sur">Naga</a> on May 6.<sup about="#mwt1062" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-272"}}' id="cite_ref-272" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-272" id="mwCCc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCCg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCCk">[</span>268<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCCo">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwCCs">Moreno held his miting de avance in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tondo,_Manila" id="mwCCw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Tondo, Manila">Tondo, Manila</a>.<sup about="#mwt1065" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-273"}}' id="cite_ref-273" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-273" id="mwCC0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCC4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCC8">[</span>269<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCDA">]</span></span></a></sup> Pacquiao held two mitings de avance, one in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cebu_City" id="mwCDE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cebu City">Cebu City</a> on May 6 and another in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/General_Santos" id="mwCDI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="General Santos">General Santos</a> on May 7.<sup about="#mwt1068" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-274"}}' id="cite_ref-274" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-274" id="mwCDM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCDQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCDU">[</span>270<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCDY">]</span></span></a></sup> The Lacson campaign held their miting de avance in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carmona,_Cavite" id="mwCDc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Carmona, Cavite">Carmona, Cavite</a> on May 6.<sup about="#mwt1071" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-275"}}' id="cite_ref-275" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-275" id="mwCDg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCDk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCDo">[</span>271<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCDs">]</span></span></a></sup> They originally planned to hold a miting de avance in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Plaza_Miranda" id="mwCDw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Plaza Miranda">Plaza Miranda</a> on May 7, but cancelled it due to time constraints.<sup about="#mwt1074" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-276"}}' id="cite_ref-276" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-276" id="mwCD0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCD4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCD8">[</span>272<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCEA">]</span></span></a></sup> De Guzman held his miting de avance on May 4 at the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quezon_Memorial_Circle" id="mwCEE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Quezon Memorial Circle">Quezon Memorial Circle</a> in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quezon_City" id="mwCEI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Quezon City">Quezon City</a>.<sup about="#mwt1077" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-277"}}' id="cite_ref-277" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-277" id="mwCEM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCEQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCEU">[</span>273<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCEY">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Campaign_donations" data-mw-section-id="23" id="mwCEc"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Campaign_donations">Campaign donations</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=23" title="Edit section: Campaign donations"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwCEg">According to their respective statements of campaign contributions, Bongbong Marcos and Sara Duterte got tens of millions of pesos in campaign contributions from contractors, in spite of an election ban on taking donations from firms that do business with the government. Rudhil Construction &amp; Enterprises owner Rodulfo Hilot Jr. gave ₱20 million to Marcos's campaign, while Quirante Construction owner Jonathan Quirante contributed ₱1 million. Rudhil Construction &amp; Enterprises and Quirante Construction were awarded substantial government contracts after the elections. Esdevco Realty Corporation, owned by Glenn Escandor, paid for advertisements worth ₱19.9 million for Sara Duterte's campaign in 2022. Genesis88 Construction, also owned by Escandor, was a major contractor for the Department of Public Works and Highways during the presidency of Rodrigo Duterte.<sup about="#mwt1080" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":02"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-:02-278"}}' id="cite_ref-:02_278-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:02-278" id="mwCEk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCEo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCEs">[</span>274<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCEw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section></section><section aria-labelledby="Results" data-mw-section-id="24" id="mwCE0"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Results">Results</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=24" title="Edit section: Results"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt1081" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Congressional canvass for the 2022 Philippine presidential election"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwCE4" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt1082" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt1081" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwCE8" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congressional_canvass_for_the_2022_Philippine_presidential_election" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Congressional canvass for the 2022 Philippine presidential election">Congressional canvass for the 2022 Philippine presidential election</a></div><p id="mwCFA">

Since the first automated election <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2010_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwCFE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2010 Philippine presidential election">in 2010</a>, preliminary results have been known overnight. The official results were canvassed by the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congress_of_the_Philippines" id="mwCFI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Congress of the Philippines">Congress of the Philippines</a> in the record time of two days. Bongbong Marcos was proclaimed president, while <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_City" id="mwCFM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao City">Davao City</a> Mayor Sara Duterte was proclaimed vice president, in a joint session at the Batasang Pambansa Complex on May 25.<sup about="#mwt1086" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"proclamation-may25"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-proclamation-may25-6"}}' id="cite_ref-proclamation-may25_6-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-proclamation-may25-6" id="mwCFQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCFU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCFY">[</span>5<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCFc">]</span></span></a></sup> All but two certificates of canvass were canvassed; those from <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Argentina" id="mwCFg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Argentina">Argentina</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Syria" id="mwCFk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Syria">Syria</a> were not included in time, as Congress deemed the votes from those certificates won't change the result.<sup about="#mwt1089" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-279"}}' id="cite_ref-279" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-279" id="mwCFo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCFs"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCFw">[</span>275<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCF0">]</span></span></a></sup></p><figure id="mwCF4" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2022_Philippine_Presidential_Election_Results_Municipality_Map.svg" id="mwCF8"><img class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="856" data-file-type="drawing" data-file-width="512" decoding="async" height="502" id="mwCGA" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:2022_Philippine_Presidential_Election_Results_Municipality_Map.svg" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f3/2022_Philippine_Presidential_Election_Results_Municipality_Map.svg/330px-2022_Philippine_Presidential_Election_Results_Municipality_Map.svg.png" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f3/2022_Philippine_Presidential_Election_Results_Municipality_Map.svg/960px-2022_Philippine_Presidential_Election_Results_Municipality_Map.svg.png 2x" width="300"/></a><figcaption id="mwCGE">Results per municipality<br id="mwCGI"/>

<b id="mwCI8">Mangondato</b><br id="mwCJA"/>
<span about="#mwt1126" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"legend","href":"./Template:Legend"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"#FFCC51"},"2":{"wt":"30–40%"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwCJE" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt1127" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Legend/styles.css"}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r981673959" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt1126" class="legend" id="mwCJI"><span class="legend-color mw-no-invert" style="background-color:#FFCC51; color:black;-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; print-color-adjust: exact;"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span></span><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>30–40%</div>
<span about="#mwt1128" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"legend","href":"./Template:Legend"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"#FFC000"},"2":{"wt":"40–50%"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwCJM" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt1129" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Legend/styles.css"}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r981673959" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt1128" class="legend" id="mwCJQ"><span class="legend-color mw-no-invert" style="background-color:#FFC000; color:black;-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; print-color-adjust: exact;"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span></span><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>40–50%</div>
<span about="#mwt1130" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"legend","href":"./Template:Legend"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"#D19800"},"2":{"wt":"50–60%"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwCJU" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt1131" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Legend/styles.css"}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r981673959" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt1130" class="legend" id="mwCJY"><span class="legend-color mw-no-invert" style="background-color:#D19800; color:black;-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; print-color-adjust: exact;"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span></span><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>50–60%</div>
<span about="#mwt1132" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"legend","href":"./Template:Legend"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"#b38400"},"2":{"wt":"60–70%"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwCJc" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt1133" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Legend/styles.css"}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r981673959" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt1132" class="legend" id="mwCJg"><span class="legend-color mw-no-invert" style="background-color:#b38400; color:black;-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; print-color-adjust: exact;"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span></span><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>60–70%</div>
<span about="#mwt1134" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"legend","href":"./Template:Legend"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"#967000"},"2":{"wt":"70–80%"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwCJk" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt1135" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Legend/styles.css"}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r981673959" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt1134" class="legend" id="mwCJo"><span class="legend-color mw-no-invert" style="background-color:#967000; color:black;-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; print-color-adjust: exact;"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span></span><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>70–80%</div>
</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mwCJs">The election broke several records. 56,095,234 citizens cast votes, an unprecedented number. <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwCJw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a> became the first candidate in the history of the Fifth Republic to win by a majority, scoring nearly 59 percent of the vote. This was the largest majority since <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1981_Philippine_presidential_election_and_referendum" id="mwCJ0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1981 Philippine presidential election and referendum">1981</a> (surpassing <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ferdinand_Marcos" id="mwCJ4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ferdinand Marcos">his father's</a> 18,309,360 votes, 88 percent of the total); as the opposition boycotted that election, it was the largest majority since <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1969_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwCJ8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1969 Philippine presidential election">1969</a> for a competitive election. His margin of almost 31 percentage points, a ratio of more than two to one over his nearest rival was the most lopsided since <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1953_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwCKA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1953 Philippine presidential election">1953</a>, when <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ramon_Magsaysay" id="mwCKE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ramon Magsaysay">Ramon Magsaysay</a> defeated the incumbent President <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elpidio_Quirino" id="mwCKI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Elpidio Quirino">Elpidio Quirino</a> by a margin of 38 percentage points. His 31,629,783 votes was not only the highest count ever recorded in a presidential election, but close to the sum total of the two previous records combined.</p>
<p id="mwCKM">Marcos won in most of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luzon" id="mwCKQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Luzon">Luzon</a>, including the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Solid_North" id="mwCKU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Solid North">Solid North</a> except for Batanes, and most of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lingayen–Lucena_corridor" id="mwCKY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Lingayen–Lucena corridor">Lingayen–Lucena corridor</a>, including <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metro_Manila" id="mwCKc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Metro Manila">Metro Manila</a>, except Quezon; he also won in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_Visayas" id="mwCKg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Central Visayas">Central Visayas</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leyte" id="mwCKk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leyte">Leyte island</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aklan" id="mwCKo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Aklan">Aklan</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bacolod" id="mwCKs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bacolod">Bacolod</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Samar_(province)" id="mwCKw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Samar (province)">Samar</a> in the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visayas" id="mwCK0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Visayas">Visayas</a>, and most of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mindanao" id="mwCK4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mindanao">Mindanao</a> except Lanao del Sur, Sarangani and the Special Geographic Area of Bangsamoro.<sup about="#mwt1138" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Kurt Dela"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Kurt_Dela-280"}}' id="cite_ref-Kurt_Dela_280-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Kurt_Dela-280" id="mwCK8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCLA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCLE">[</span>276<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCLI">]</span></span></a></sup> Marcos also won among detainee voters, and in local and overseas absentee voting.<sup about="#mwt1141" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Abad"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Abad-281"}}' id="cite_ref-Abad_281-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Abad-281" id="mwCLM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCLQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCLU">[</span>277<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCLY">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwCLc">Robredo won in her home region of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bicol_Region" id="mwCLg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bicol Region">Bicol</a>, and in neighboring <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quezon" id="mwCLk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Quezon">Quezon</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Batanes" id="mwCLo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Batanes">Batanes</a>, and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Western_Visayas" id="mwCLs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Western Visayas">Western Visayas</a> except Aklan and Bacolod, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Northern_Samar" id="mwCLw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Northern Samar">Northern</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eastern_Samar" id="mwCL0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Eastern Samar">Eastern Samar</a>, and in the <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special_geographic_area_of_Bangsamoro" id="mwCL4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Special geographic area of Bangsamoro">Special Geographic Area of Bangsamoro</a>.<sup about="#mwt1142" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Kurt Dela"}}' id="cite_ref-Kurt_Dela_280-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Kurt_Dela-280" id="mwCL8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCMA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCME">[</span>276<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCMI">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwCMM">Pacquiao won in his adopted home province of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sarangani" id="mwCMQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sarangani">Sarangani</a>, while Mangondato won in his home province of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lanao_del_Sur" id="mwCMU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Lanao del Sur">Lanao del Sur</a>. No other candidates won a province or city canvassed by Congress. Most notably, Moreno failed to carry his home city of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manila" id="mwCMY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Manila">Manila</a>, where he was the incumbent mayor, while Lacson failed to carry his home province of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cavite" id="mwCMc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cavite">Cavite</a> or even his hometown of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Imus" id="mwCMg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Imus">Imus</a>.<sup about="#mwt1145" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Pena2022"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Pena2022-282"}}' id="cite_ref-Pena2022_282-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Pena2022-282" id="mwCMk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCMo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCMs">[</span>278<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCMw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>

<div about="#mwt1152" class="mw-references-wrap" data-mw='{"name":"references","attrs":{"group":"lower-alpha","responsive":"1"},"body":{"html":""}}' id="mwCNc" typeof="mw:Extension/references"><ol class="mw-references references" data-mw-group="lower-alpha" id="mwCNg"><li about="#cite_note-lpind-1" data-mw-footnote-number="a" id="cite_note-lpind-1"><span class="mw-cite-backlink" id="mwCNk"><a data-mw-group="lower-alpha" href="#cite_ref-lpind_1-0" id="mwCNo" rel="mw:referencedBy"><span class="mw-linkback-text" id="mwCNs">↑</span></a></span> <span class="mw-reference-text reference-text" data-mw-group="lower-alpha" id="mw-reference-text-cite_note-lpind-1"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Liberal_Party_(Philippines)" id="mwCNw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Liberal Party (Philippines)">Liberal Party</a> member running as an independent</span></li>
<li about="#cite_note-plind-115" data-mw-footnote-number="b" id="cite_note-plind-115"><span class="mw-cite-backlink" id="mwCN0" rel="mw:referencedBy"><a data-mw-group="lower-alpha" href="#cite_ref-plind_115-0" id="mwCN4"><span class="mw-linkback-text" id="mwCN8">1</span></a> <a data-mw-group="lower-alpha" href="#cite_ref-plind_115-1" id="mwCOA"><span class="mw-linkback-text" id="mwCOE">2</span></a></span> <span class="mw-reference-text reference-text" data-mw-group="lower-alpha" id="mw-reference-text-cite_note-plind-115">Originally ran under <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Partido_para_sa_Demokratikong_Reporma" id="mwCOI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Partido para sa Demokratikong Reporma">Partido para sa Demokratikong Reporma</a>; resigned from the party mid-way through the campaign. Still labeled as a Reporma candidate on the official ballots.</span></li>

<li about="#cite_note-200" data-mw-footnote-number="d" id="cite_note-200"><span class="mw-cite-backlink" id="mwCOk"><a data-mw-group="lower-alpha" href="#cite_ref-200" id="mwCOo" rel="mw:referencedBy"><span class="mw-linkback-text" id="mwCOs">↑</span></a></span> <span class="mw-reference-text reference-text" data-mw-group="lower-alpha" id="mw-reference-text-cite_note-200">Originally ran under <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Partido_para_sa_Demokratikong_Reporma" id="mwCOw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Partido para sa Demokratikong Reporma">Partido para sa Demokratikong Reporma</a>; resigned from the party mid-way through the campaign. <td id="mwCm0"><b id="mwCm4">6.48</b></td>
<td id="mwCm8"><b id="mwCnA">53,815,484</b></td>
<td id="mwCnE"><b id="mwCnI">100</b></td></tr>
</tbody></table>
<ul id="mwCnM"><li id="mwCnQ">Note: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basilan" id="mwCnU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Basilan">Basilan</a> is divided by two regions: its capital city <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isabela,_Basilan" id="mwCnY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Isabela, Basilan">Isabela</a> is a part of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zamboanga_Peninsula" id="mwCnc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Zamboanga Peninsula">Zamboanga Peninsula</a> (Region IX), while the rest of its municipalities are in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bangsamoro" id="mwCng" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bangsamoro">Bangsamoro</a> (BARMM). Its entire totals are included in Bangsamoro.</li></ul>
