</section></section></section><section aria-labelledby="Vice_presidential_election" data-mw-section-id="27" id="mwCpA"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Vice_presidential_election">Vice presidential election</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=27" title="Edit section: Vice presidential election"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<section aria-labelledby="Candidates_2" data-mw-section-id="28" id="mwCpE"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Candidates_2">Candidates</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=28" title="Edit section: Candidates"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<section aria-labelledby="Lito_Atienza_(PROMDI)" data-mw-section-id="29" id="mwCpI"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Lito_Atienza_(PROMDI)"><span id="Lito_Atienza_.28PROMDI.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Lito Atienza (PROMDI)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=29" title="Edit section: Lito Atienza (PROMDI)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwCpM"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buhay_Party-List" id="mwCpQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Buhay Party-List">Buhay Party-List</a> representative <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lito_Atienza" id="mwCpU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Lito Atienza">Lito Atienza</a> was chosen by Pacquiao as his vice presidential running mate.<sup about="#mwt1252" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-283"}}' id="cite_ref-283" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-283" id="mwCpY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCpc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCpg">[</span>279<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCpk">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Walden_Bello_(PLM)" data-mw-section-id="30" id="mwCpo"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Walden_Bello_(PLM)"><span id="Walden_Bello_.28PLM.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Walden Bello (PLM)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=30" title="Edit section: Walden Bello (PLM)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwCps">The <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laban_ng_Masa" id="mwCpw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Laban ng Masa">Laban ng Masa</a> (<abbr about="#mwt1253" data-mw='{"attribs":[[{"txt":"title"},{"html":"&lt;span typeof=\"mw:Nowiki\" id=\"mwCp0\"&gt;translation&lt;/span&gt;"}]],"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"Translation","href":"./Template:Translation"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Struggle of the Masses"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwCp4" title="translation" typeof="mw:Transclusion mw:ExpandedAttrs">transl.</abbr><span about="#mwt1253" id="mwCp8"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span>Struggle of the Masses</span>) coalition launched a campaign to collect 300,000 signatures to urge activist and former party-list lawmaker <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Walden_Bello" id="mwCqA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Walden Bello">Walden Bello</a> to run for president in the 2022 elections. In a statement, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laban_ng_Masa" id="mwCqE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Laban ng Masa">Laban ng Masa</a> said it wanted to "push for an ambitious platform that focuses on the poor, prioritizes the neglected, and fights for the rights of ordinary Filipinos."<sup about="#mwt1258" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-284"}}' id="cite_ref-284" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-284" id="mwCqI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCqM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCqQ">[</span>280<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCqU">]</span></span></a></sup> Bello's group sought talks with Vice President Robredo's backers for three months but were ignored. This caused them to support <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leody_de_Guzman" id="mwCqY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Leody de Guzman">Leody de Guzman</a>'s presidential candidacy, instead.<sup about="#mwt1262" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-285"}}' id="cite_ref-285" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-285" id="mwCqc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCqg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCqk">[</span>281<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCqo">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwCqs">Later in October 20, Bello decided to run for the vice presidency, substituting Raquel Castillo who was supposed to be de Guzman's running mate.<sup about="#mwt1265" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Inquirer-Bello-VP"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-Inquirer-Bello-VP-286"}}' id="cite_ref-Inquirer-Bello-VP_286-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Inquirer-Bello-VP-286" id="mwCqw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCq0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCq4">[</span>282<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCq8">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Rizalito_David_(DPP)" data-mw-section-id="31" id="mwCrA"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Rizalito_David_(DPP)"><span id="Rizalito_David_.28DPP.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Rizalito David (DPP)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=31" title="Edit section: Rizalito David (DPP)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwCrE">David filed his candidacy on October 8.<sup about="#mwt1269" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"filing8"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-filing8-287"}}' id="cite_ref-filing8_287-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-filing8-287" id="mwCrI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCrM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCrQ">[</span>283<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCrU">]</span></span></a></sup> David was most notable for losing a <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/David_v._Poe" id="mwCrY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="David v. Poe"><i id="mwCrc">quo warranto</i> petition</a> against <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Poe" id="mwCrg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Grace Poe">Grace Poe</a> in the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2016_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwCrk" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2016 Philippine presidential election">2016 election</a>.<sup about="#mwt1272" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-288"}}' id="cite_ref-288" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-288" id="mwCro"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCrs"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCrw">[</span>284<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCr0">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Sara_Duterte_(Lakas)" data-mw-section-id="32" id="mwCr4"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Sara_Duterte_(Lakas)"><span id="Sara_Duterte_.28Lakas.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Sara Duterte (Lakas)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=32" title="Edit section: Sara Duterte (Lakas)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwCr8">On July 9, 2021, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Davao_City" id="mwCsA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Davao City">Davao City</a> mayor <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sara_Duterte" id="mwCsE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sara Duterte">Sara Duterte</a> said that she was open to running for president. However, there was no final decision yet.<sup about="#mwt1273" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":19"}}' id="cite_ref-:19_77-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:19-77" id="mwCsI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCsM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCsQ">[</span>76<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCsU">]</span></span></a></sup> On September 9, 2021, she said that she was not running for president since her father, President Duterte was running for vice president, and they had agreed that only one of them would run for a national position.<sup about="#mwt1276" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-289"}}' id="cite_ref-289" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-289" id="mwCsY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCsc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCsg">[</span>285<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCsk">]</span></span></a></sup> On November 11, she resigned from <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hugpong_ng_Pagbabago" id="mwCso" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Hugpong ng Pagbabago">Hugpong ng Pagbabago</a> and joined <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lakas–CMD" id="mwCss" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Lakas–CMD">Lakas–CMD</a> the same day.<sup about="#mwt1277" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":21"}}' id="cite_ref-:21_81-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:21-81" id="mwCsw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCs0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCs4">[</span>80<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCs8">]</span></span></a></sup> She filed her candidacy on November 13, 2021, substituting for Lyle Fernando Uy.<sup about="#mwt1278" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":22"}}' id="cite_ref-:22_82-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:22-82" id="mwCtA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCtE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCtI">[</span>81<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCtM">]</span></span></a></sup> <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Partido_Federal_ng_Pilipinas" id="mwCtQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Partido Federal ng Pilipinas">Partido Federal ng Pilipinas</a> adopted Duterte as their vice presidential candidate and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwCtU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bongbong Marcos">Bongbong Marcos</a>'s running mate.<sup about="#mwt1279" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":23"}}' id="cite_ref-:23_83-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:23-83" id="mwCtY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCtc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCtg">[</span>82<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCtk">]</span></span></a></sup> Lakas and Duterte then announced that they were supporting Marcos's presidential bid; PDP–Laban first turned down her appeal for support.<sup about="#mwt1282" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-290"}}' id="cite_ref-290" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-290" id="mwCto"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCts"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCtw">[</span>286<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCt0">]</span></span></a></sup> Later, on March 22, PDP-Laban endorsed <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marcos_Jr." id="mwCt4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Marcos Jr.">Marcos Jr.</a>, but <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_Duterte" id="mwCt8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="President Duterte">President Duterte</a> chose to remain neutral.<sup about="#mwt1285" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-291"}}' id="cite_ref-291" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-291" id="mwCuA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCuE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCuI">[</span>287<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCuM">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Manny_Lopez_(WPP)" data-mw-section-id="33" id="mwCuQ"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Manny_Lopez_(WPP)"><span id="Manny_Lopez_.28WPP.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Manny Lopez (WPP)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=33" title="Edit section: Manny Lopez (WPP)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwCuU"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manny_SD_Lopez" id="mwCuY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Manny SD Lopez">Manny SD Lopez</a> said that if elected vice president, he'd prefer to head the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Department_of_Foreign_Affairs_(Philippines)" id="mwCuc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Department of Foreign Affairs (Philippines)">Department of Foreign Affairs</a> or the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Department_of_Trade_and_Industry_(Philippines)" id="mwCug" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Department of Trade and Industry (Philippines)">Department of Trade and Industry</a>.<sup about="#mwt1288" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-292"}}' id="cite_ref-292" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-292" id="mwCuk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCuo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCus">[</span>288<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCuw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Willie_Ong_(Aksyon)" data-mw-section-id="34" id="mwCu0"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Willie_Ong_(Aksyon)"><span id="Willie_Ong_.28Aksyon.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Willie Ong (Aksyon)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=34" title="Edit section: Willie Ong (Aksyon)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwCu4">Ong was the running mate of Moreno; their ticket was officially announced on September 22, 2021.<sup about="#mwt1289" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"isko ong2"}}' id="cite_ref-isko_ong2_64-2" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-isko_ong2-64" id="mwCu8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCvA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCvE">[</span>63<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCvI">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Francis_Pangilinan_(Liberal)" data-mw-section-id="35" id="mwCvM"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Francis_Pangilinan_(Liberal)"><span id="Francis_Pangilinan_.28Liberal.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Francis Pangilinan (Liberal)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=35" title="Edit section: Francis Pangilinan (Liberal)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwCvQ">In June 2021, Pangilinan announced that he was seeking reelection to the Senate.<sup about="#mwt1293" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-293"}}' id="cite_ref-293" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-293" id="mwCvU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCvY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCvc">[</span>289<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCvg">]</span></span></a></sup> After Vice President Robredo announced her presidential candidacy, several sources from the Liberal Party indicated that the Senator would be her running mate for her presidential bid.<sup about="#mwt1294" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":10"}}' id="cite_ref-:10_69-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:10-69" id="mwCvk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCvo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCvs">[</span>68<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCvw">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt1295" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":11"}}' id="cite_ref-:11_70-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:11-70" id="mwCv0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCv4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCv8">[</span>69<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCwA">]</span></span></a></sup> Pangilinan filed his candidacy for vice president a day after Robredo.<sup about="#mwt1296" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":":12"}}' id="cite_ref-:12_71-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-:12-71" id="mwCwE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCwI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCwM">[</span>70<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCwQ">]</span></span></a></sup> His campaign platform was focused on food security and agriculture.</p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Carlos_Serapio_(Katipunan)" data-mw-section-id="36" id="mwCwU"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Carlos_Serapio_(Katipunan)"><span id="Carlos_Serapio_.28Katipunan.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Carlos Serapio (Katipunan)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=36" title="Edit section: Carlos Serapio (Katipunan)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwCwY"><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carlos_Serapio" id="mwCwc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Carlos Serapio">Carlos Serapio</a>, Magondato's running mate, said that he would push for <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federalism_in_the_Philippines" id="mwCwg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Federalism in the Philippines">federalism in the Philippines</a> if he won.<sup about="#mwt1299" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-294"}}' id="cite_ref-294" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-294" id="mwCwk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCwo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCws">[</span>290<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCww">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Tito_Sotto_(NPC)" data-mw-section-id="37" id="mwCw0"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Tito_Sotto_(NPC)"><span id="Tito_Sotto_.28NPC.29" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Tito Sotto (NPC)</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=37" title="Edit section: Tito Sotto (NPC)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwCw4">In July 2021, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tito_Sotto" id="mwCw8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Tito Sotto">Tito Sotto</a> announced that he would be Lacson's eventual running mate in the presidential race;<sup about="#mwt1300" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"lacson-sotto"}}' id="cite_ref-lacson-sotto_145-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-lacson-sotto-145" id="mwCxA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCxE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCxI">[</span>142<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCxM">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt1301" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"pingtito"}}' id="cite_ref-pingtito_146-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-pingtito-146" id="mwCxQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCxU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCxY">[</span>143<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCxc">]</span></span></a></sup> this was followed by an official campaign announcement on September 8, 2021.<sup about="#mwt1302" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"lacsonsotto"}}' id="cite_ref-lacsonsotto_147-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-lacsonsotto-147" id="mwCxg"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwCxk"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCxo">[</span>144<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwCxs">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section></section><section aria-labelledby="Debates_and_forums_2" data-mw-section-id="38" id="mwCxw"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Debates_and_forums_2">Debates and forums</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=38" title="Edit section: Debates and forums"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>

</tbody></table>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Opinion_polls" data-mw-section-id="39" id="mwC0g"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Opinion_polls">Opinion polls</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=39" title="Edit section: Opinion polls"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt1335" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"main","href":"./Template:Main"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Opinion polling for the 2022 Philippine presidential election"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwC0k" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt1336" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Hatnote/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1353705441" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt1335" class="hatnote navigation-not-searchable" id="mwC0o" role="note">Main article: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Opinion_polling_for_the_2022_Philippine_presidential_election" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Opinion polling for the 2022 Philippine presidential election">Opinion polling for the 2022 Philippine presidential election</a></div>
<p id="mwC0s"><a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Opinion_polling" id="mwC0w" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Opinion polling">Opinion polling</a>, commonly known as "surveys" in the Philippines, is conducted by <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Social_Weather_Stations" id="mwC00" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Social Weather Stations">Social Weather Stations</a> (SWS), <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pulse_Asia" id="mwC04" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pulse Asia">Pulse Asia</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/OCTA_Research" id="mwC08" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="OCTA Research">OCTA Research</a>, and other third-party pollsters.</p>
<p id="mwC1A">The tables below the latest six polls that were administered.</p>
<span about="#mwt1337" class="mw-empty-elt" data-mw='{"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"wide image","href":"./Template:Wide_image"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Opinion polls for Philippine vice presidential election 2022.svg"},"2":{"wt":"950px"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwC1E" typeof="mw:Transclusion"><link about="#mwt1338" data-mw='{"name":"templatestyles","attrs":{"src":"Module:Wide image/styles.css"},"body":{"extsrc":""}}' href="mw-data:TemplateStyles:r1330227556" rel="mw-deduplicated-inline-style" typeof="mw:Extension/templatestyles"/></span><div about="#mwt1337" class="thumb tnone" id="mwC1I" style="margin-left:auto;margin-right:auto;overflow:hidden;width:auto;max-width:960px"><div class="thumbinner"><div class="noresize thumbimage" style="overflow:auto"><span data-mw='{"caption":""}' typeof="mw:File"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Opinion_polls_for_Philippine_vice_presidential_election_2022.svg"><img alt="" class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="228" data-file-type="drawing" data-file-width="512" decoding="async" height="423" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Opinion_polls_for_Philippine_vice_presidential_election_2022.svg" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/dc/Opinion_polls_for_Philippine_vice_presidential_election_2022.svg/960px-Opinion_polls_for_Philippine_vice_presidential_election_2022.svg.png" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/dc/Opinion_polls_for_Philippine_vice_presidential_election_2022.svg/1920px-Opinion_polls_for_Philippine_vice_presidential_election_2022.svg.png 2x" width="950"/></a></span></div></div></div>

</section><section aria-labelledby="Results_2" data-mw-section-id="40" id="mwC24"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Results_2">Results</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=40" title="Edit section: Results"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwC28">Just like her running mate, Duterte won with the largest majority of the Fifth Republic, and the largest majority since <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1969_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwC3A" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1969 Philippine presidential election">1969</a>. Duterte's vote count exceeded that of Marcos, and she got the most votes by a Filipino for any office in history. Her 43.71% winning margin is the largest in history since <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sergio_Osmeña" id="mwC3E" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sergio Osmeña">Sergio Osmeña</a>'s landslide victories in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1935_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwC3I" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1935 Philippine presidential election">1935</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1941_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwC3M" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="1941 Philippine presidential election">1941</a>.</p>
<p id="mwC3Q">Duterte won throughout the country except in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guimaras" id="mwC3U" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Guimaras">Guimaras</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iloilo" id="mwC3Y" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Iloilo">Iloilo</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iloilo_City" id="mwC3c" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Iloilo City">Iloilo City</a>, and in the Bicol provinces of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Albay" id="mwC3g" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Albay">Albay</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camarines_Norte" id="mwC3k" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Camarines Norte">Camarines Norte</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Camarines_Sur" id="mwC3o" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Camarines Sur">Camarines Sur</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Catanduanes" id="mwC3s" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Catanduanes">Catanduanes</a>, where Robredo's running mate Francis Pangilinan won, and in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sorsogon" id="mwC3w" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Sorsogon">Sorsogon</a> where Tito Sotto won. Duterte won in all areas in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mindanao" id="mwC30" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Mindanao">Mindanao</a> canvassed by Congress.<sup about="#mwt1355" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Pena2022"}}' id="cite_ref-Pena2022_282-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Pena2022-282" id="mwC34"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwC38"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwC4A">[</span>278<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwC4E">]</span></span></a></sup> Among absentee voters, Duterte won amongst the local and overseas voters,<sup about="#mwt1356" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Abad"}}' id="cite_ref-Abad_281-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Abad-281" id="mwC4I"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwC4M"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwC4Q">[</span>277<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwC4U">]</span></span></a></sup> while Pangilinan nosed her out among detainee voters.<sup about="#mwt1359" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-298"}}' id="cite_ref-298" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-298" id="mwC4Y"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwC4c"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwC4g">[</span>294<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwC4k">]</span></span></a></sup></p>

<ul id="mwDSk"><li id="mwDSo">Note: <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basilan" id="mwDSs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Basilan">Basilan</a> is divided by two regions: its capital city <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Isabela,_Basilan" id="mwDSw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Isabela, Basilan">Isabela</a> is a part of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zamboanga_Peninsula" id="mwDS0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Zamboanga Peninsula">Zamboanga Peninsula</a> (Region IX), while the rest of its municipalities are in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bangsamoro" id="mwDS4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bangsamoro">Bangsamoro</a> (BARMM). Its entire totals are included in Bangsamoro.</li></ul>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Results_per_province,_city,_diplomatic_post_and_absentee_voting_category" data-mw-section-id="42" id="mwDS8"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Results_per_province,_city,_diplomatic_post_and_absentee_voting_category"><span id="Results_per_province.2C_city.2C_diplomatic_post_and_absentee_voting_category" typeof="mw:FallbackId"></span>Results per province, city, diplomatic post and absentee voting category</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=42" title="Edit section: Results per province, city, diplomatic post and absentee voting category"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwDTA">Each <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Provinces_of_the_Philippines" id="mwDTE" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Provinces of the Philippines">province</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cities_of_the_Philippines" id="mwDTI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cities of the Philippines">city</a> that is <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Highly_urbanized_city" id="mwDTM" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Highly urbanized city">independent of a province</a> and is a <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congressional_districts_of_the_Philippines" id="mwDTQ" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Congressional districts of the Philippines">congressional district</a> by itself sent its certificates of canvass to Congress. Each diplomatic post, local absentee voters and detainee voters also sent their respective certificates of canvass. The <a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special_geographic_area_of_Bangsamoro" id="mwDTU" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Special geographic area of Bangsamoro">Special Geographic Area of Bangsamoro</a>, and jointly <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taguig" id="mwDTY" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Taguig">Taguig</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pateros" id="mwDTc" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Pateros">Pateros</a> sent their respective certificates of canvass. From there, the results were tallied in <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congressional_canvass_for_the_2022_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwDTg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Congressional canvass for the 2022 Philippine presidential election">a joint session of Congress</a>.</p>

</section><section aria-labelledby="Close_places" data-mw-section-id="43" id="mwDXI"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading4"><h4 id="Close_places">Close places</h4><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=43" title="Edit section: Close places"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<span about="#mwt1424" class="mw-empty-elt" <p>Margin of victory is less than 5% for the presidential election:</p>
<ul><li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aklan" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Aklan">Aklan</a>: 0.01% (Marcos wins by 39 votes)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marinduque" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Marinduque">Marinduque</a>: 0.67% (Marcos win)</li>
<li>Detainee voters: 0.98% (Marcos wins by one vote)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indonesia" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Indonesia">Indonesia</a>: 0.98% (Marcos win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Agusan_del_Sur" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Agusan del Sur">Agusan del Sur</a>: 2.37% (Marcos win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oriental_Mindoro" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Oriental Mindoro">Oriental Mindoro</a>: 3.09% (Marcos win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bacolod" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Bacolod">Bacolod</a>: 3.26% (Marcos win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Australia" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Australia">Australia</a>: 3.43% (Robredo win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Romblon" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Romblon">Romblon</a>: 3.59% (Marcos win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Negros_Occidental" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Negros Occidental">Negros Occidental</a>: 3.60% (Robredo win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="United States">United States</a>: 3.86% (Marcos win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Batangas" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Batangas">Batangas</a>: 4.11% (Marcos win)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Negros_Oriental" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Negros Oriental">Negros Oriental</a>: 4.87% (Marcos win)</li></ul></td>
<td class="col-break col-break-2">
<p>Margin of victory is less than 5% for the vice presidential election:</p>
<ul><li>Detainee voters: 1.04% (Pangilinan wins by one vote)</li>
<li><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antique_(province)" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Antique (province)">Antique</a>: 2.67% (Duterte win)</li>

<li><a class="mw-redirect" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quezon_(province)" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Quezon (province)">Quezon</a>: 3.71% (Duterte win)</li></ul>

</section></section></section><section aria-labelledby="Aftermath" data-mw-section-id="45" id="mwDXg"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Aftermath">Aftermath</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=45" title="Edit section: Aftermath"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<section aria-labelledby="Election_night" data-mw-section-id="46" id="mwDXk"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Election_night">Election night</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=46" title="Edit section: Election night"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwDXo">Despite calls to extend voting hours due to technical difficulties with the vote-counting machines (VCMs), the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Commission_on_Elections_(Philippines)" id="mwDXs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Commission on Elections (Philippines)">COMELEC</a> closed voting at 7:00 pm, although they allowed those within 30 meters of the polling precinct by that time to cast their votes.<sup about="#mwt1436" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-302"}}' id="cite_ref-302" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-302" id="mwDXw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDX0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDX4">[</span>295<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDX8">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwDYA">Transmission of election returns began shortly after closing. Within four hours, about 80,000 returns were transmitted to the commission, an improvement from 2016 and 2019 where it took ten and eleven hours respectively.<sup about="#mwt1439" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"fasttrans"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-fasttrans-303"}}' id="cite_ref-fasttrans_303-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-fasttrans-303" id="mwDYE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDYI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDYM">[</span>296<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDYQ">]</span></span></a></sup> The COMELEC attributed the speed to improvements in the facilities, the training of workers and technicians, and the services by telecommunication companies.<sup about="#mwt1440" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"fasttrans"}}' id="cite_ref-fasttrans_303-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-fasttrans-303" id="mwDYU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDYY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDYc">[</span>296<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDYg">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt1443" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-304"}}' id="cite_ref-304" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-304" id="mwDYk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDYo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDYs">[</span>297<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDYw">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwDY0">At around 11 pm, three hours after initial results of the elections were released, Marcos made a short speech thanking his supporters for voting for him. Despite leading in the preliminary results, he acknowledged that the counting was not yet over. He also urged his supporters to be vigilant and watch their votes.<sup about="#mwt1446" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-305"}}' id="cite_ref-305" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-305" id="mwDY4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDY8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDZA">[</span>298<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDZE">]</span></span></a></sup> Shortly after 3:00 am, Robredo issued a statement to thank her supporters, and called their campaign "historic" for being led by volunteers. She reiterated that she would not back down, noting that not all votes have been counted yet and that other issues had yet to be resolved, but called on her supporters to listen to the voice of the electorate.<sup about="#mwt1449" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-306"}}' id="cite_ref-306" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-306" id="mwDZI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDZM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDZQ">[</span>299<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDZU">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Concessions" data-mw-section-id="47" id="mwDZY"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Concessions">Concessions</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=47" title="Edit section: Concessions"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<figure class="mw-default-size" id="mwDZc" typeof="mw:File/Thumb"><a class="mw-file-description" href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Bongbong_Marcos_and_Leni_Robredo.jpg" id="mwDZg"><img class="mw-file-element" data-file-height="884" data-file-type="bitmap" data-file-width="1200" decoding="async" height="184" id="mwDZk" resource="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Bongbong_Marcos_and_Leni_Robredo.jpg" src="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c6/Bongbong_Marcos_and_Leni_Robredo.jpg/250px-Bongbong_Marcos_and_Leni_Robredo.jpg" srcset="//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c6/Bongbong_Marcos_and_Leni_Robredo.jpg/500px-Bongbong_Marcos_and_Leni_Robredo.jpg 2x" width="250"/></a><figcaption id="mwDZo">In October 2024, Leni Robredo and Bongbong Marcos met for the first time since the election and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Inauguration_of_Bongbong_Marcos" id="mwDZs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Inauguration of Bongbong Marcos">the latter's inauguration</a>.</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mwDZw">Isko Moreno conceded to Marcos the day after the elections. He urged his supporters to support Marcos.<sup about="#mwt1452" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-307"}}' id="cite_ref-307" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-307" id="mwDZ0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDZ4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDZ8">[</span>300<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDaA">]</span></span></a></sup> Pacquiao conceded to Marcos and bid him well wishes.<sup about="#mwt1455" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-308"}}' id="cite_ref-308" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-308" id="mwDaE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDaI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDaM">[</span>301<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDaQ">]</span></span></a></sup> Willie Ong also conceded to Duterte, wishing both her and Marcos success.<sup about="#mwt1458" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-309"}}' id="cite_ref-309" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-309" id="mwDaU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDaY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDac">[</span>302<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDag">]</span></span></a></sup>  Faisal Mangondato and Carlos Serapio also conceded, via a statement released by their political party, saying that Marcos and Duterte "obtained the People’s Mandate."<sup about="#mwt1461" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-310"}}' id="cite_ref-310" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-310" id="mwDak"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDao"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDas">[</span>303<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDaw">]</span></span></a></sup> Manny SD Lopez conceded to Duterte, saying "Whatever the Filipino electorates considered in their choice of candidates, we have to respect, for this is the essence of democracy."<sup about="#mwt1464" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-311"}}' id="cite_ref-311" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-311" id="mwDa0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDa4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDa8">[</span>304<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDbA">]</span></span></a></sup> Independent candidate Ernesto Abella also conceded several days after the election, saying he respected the results and acknowledging Marcos as the next president.<sup about="#mwt1465" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"Abella"}}' id="cite_ref-Abella_210-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-Abella-210" id="mwDbE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDbI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDbM">[</span>206<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDbQ">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwDbU">Lacson, while he did not concede, said that he was retiring to his home in Cavite. His running mate, Tito Sotto, conceded the vice presidency in a separate statement, and wished the incoming administration, without naming names, "good luck."<sup about="#mwt1468" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-312"}}' id="cite_ref-312" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-312" id="mwDbY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDbc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDbg">[</span>305<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDbk">]</span></span></a></sup> In a thanksgiving event at the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ateneo_de_Manila_University" id="mwDbo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Ateneo de Manila University">Ateneo de Manila University</a>, Robredo and Pangilinan urged their supporters to accept the results of the election, while not explicitly conceding.<sup about="#mwt1471" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-313"}}' id="cite_ref-313" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-313" id="mwDbs"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDbw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDb0">[</span>306<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDb4">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwDb8">Leody de Guzman, while citing irregularities on election day, conceded;<sup about="#mwt1474" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-314"}}' id="cite_ref-314" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-314" id="mwDcA"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDcE"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDcI">[</span>307<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDcM">]</span></span></a></sup> his running mate, Walden Bello, slammed Marcos, saying the country may face "six years of instability" and that a large part of the country will "refuse to grant legitimacy and credibility to the rule of a family of thieves".<sup about="#mwt1477" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-315"}}' id="cite_ref-315" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-315" id="mwDcQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDcU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDcY">[</span>308<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDcc">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="Protests" data-mw-section-id="48" id="mwDcg"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="Protests">Protests</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=48" title="Edit section: Protests"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwDck">On May 10, several groups protested in front of the COMELEC main office at the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palacio_del_Gobernador" id="mwDco" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Palacio del Gobernador">Palacio del Gobernador</a> in Intramuros.<sup about="#mwt1482" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-316"}}' id="cite_ref-316" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-316" id="mwDcs"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDcw"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDc0">[</span>309<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDc4">]</span></span></a></sup> They alleged massive <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electoral_fraud" id="mwDc8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Electoral fraud">electoral fraud</a>, citing reports of <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2022_Philippine_general_election#Issues_with_vote-counting_machines" id="mwDdA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="2022 Philippine general election">1,800 defective or malfunctioning VCMs</a>, the voters being asked to leave their ballots at the precinct for mass feeding after some VCMs broke down, and the fast transmission of results. Protesters were reportedly chanting "Marcos, Magnanakaw" (<abbr about="#mwt1478" data-mw='{"attribs":[[{"txt":"title"},{"html":"&lt;span typeof=\"mw:Nowiki\" id=\"mwDdE\"&gt;literal translation&lt;/span&gt;"}]],"parts":[{"template":{"target":{"wt":"literal translation","href":"./Template:Literal_translation"},"params":{"1":{"wt":"Marcos, Thief"}},"i":0}}]}' id="mwDdI" style="font-size:85%" title="literal translation" typeof="mw:Transclusion mw:ExpandedAttrs">lit.</abbr><span about="#mwt1478" style="white-space: nowrap;"><span typeof="mw:Entity"> </span></span><span about="#mwt1478" class="gloss-quot">'</span><span about="#mwt1478" class="gloss-text">Marcos, Thief</span><span about="#mwt1478" class="gloss-quot" id="mwDdM">'</span>), a chant popularized during the 1980s.<sup about="#mwt1485" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-317"}}' id="cite_ref-317" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-317" id="mwDdQ"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDdU"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDdY">[</span>310<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDdc">]</span></span></a></sup> Meanwhile, the Office of the Student Regent of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_the_Philippines" id="mwDdg" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="University of the Philippines">University of the Philippines</a> called on their students to walk out, announcing that there will be "no classes under a Marcos presidency."<sup about="#mwt1488" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-318"}}' id="cite_ref-318" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-318" id="mwDdk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDdo"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDds">[</span>311<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDdw">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt1491" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-319"}}' id="cite_ref-319" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-319" id="mwDd0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDd4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDd8">[</span>312<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDeA">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwDeE">The electoral commission has denied and debunked claims of electoral fraud.<sup about="#mwt1494" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-320"}}' id="cite_ref-320" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-320" id="mwDeI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDeM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDeQ">[</span>313<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDeU">]</span></span></a></sup> Election watchdog Legal Network for Truthful Elections (LENTE) disputed claims that the speedy transmission of returns was suspicious,<sup about="#mwt1497" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-321"}}' id="cite_ref-321" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-321" id="mwDeY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDec"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDeg">[</span>314<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDek">]</span></span></a></sup> while the COMELEC stated that leaving ballots behind if VCMs malfunction is part of the protocol to avoid voter disenfranchisement.<sup about="#mwt1500" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-322"}}' id="cite_ref-322" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-322" id="mwDeo"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDes"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDew">[</span>315<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDe0">]</span></span></a></sup> The <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Parish_Pastoral_Council_for_Responsible_Voting" id="mwDe4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Parish Pastoral Council for Responsible Voting">Parish Pastoral Council for Responsible Voting</a> (PPCRV) found no irregularities in the partial and unofficial count.<sup about="#mwt1503" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-323"}}' id="cite_ref-323" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-323" id="mwDe8"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDfA"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDfE">[</span>316<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDfI">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt1507" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"ppcrvno"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-ppcrvno-324"}}' id="cite_ref-ppcrvno_324-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-ppcrvno-324" id="mwDfM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDfQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDfU">[</span>317<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDfY">]</span></span></a></sup> Meanwhile, the PPCRV and other statisticians dismissed claims of a programmed consistent percentage gap between Marcos and Robredo, after a viral post in social media claimed the consistency was evidence of fraud. According to the post, at every incremental update in the counting, Robredo's count never deviated from 47 percent of the number of votes that Marcos had, a phenomenon they considered suspicious.<sup about="#mwt1508" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"ppcrvno"}}' id="cite_ref-ppcrvno_324-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-ppcrvno-324" id="mwDfc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDfg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDfk">[</span>317<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDfo">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwDfs">On May 16 and 17, 2022, respectively, two of the petitions against Marcos' candidacy that were later dismissed by Comelec <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Supreme_Court_petitions_to_nullify_Bongbong_Marcos'_candidacy_in_the_2022_Philippine_presidential_election" id="mwDfw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Supreme Court petitions to nullify Bongbong Marcos' candidacy in the 2022 Philippine presidential election">were filed post-election at the Supreme Court</a>.<sup about="#mwt1511" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-325"}}' id="cite_ref-325" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-325" id="mwDf0"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDf4"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDf8">[</span>318<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDgA">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt1514" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-326"}}' id="cite_ref-326" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-326" id="mwDgE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDgI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDgM">[</span>319<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDgQ">]</span></span></a></sup> The consolidated petitions were dismissed by the Court on June 28.<sup about="#mwt1517" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-327"}}' id="cite_ref-327" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-327" id="mwDgU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDgY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDgc">[</span>320<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDgg">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwDgk">In 2023, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eliseo_Rio_Jr." id="mwDgo" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Eliseo Rio Jr.">Eliseo Rio Jr.</a>, a former acting secretary of the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Department_of_Information_and_Communications_Technology" id="mwDgs" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Department of Information and Communications Technology">Department of Information and Communications Technology</a>, investigated along with a team of forensics experts, the transmission of election returns to the COMELEC after he had doubts about the high transmission rate, and alleged that a large portion of election returns  from <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metro_Manila" id="mwDgw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Metro Manila">Metro Manila</a>, <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cavite" id="mwDg0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Cavite">Cavite</a> and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Batangas" id="mwDg4" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Batangas">Batangas</a> were transmitted through the <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IP_address" id="mwDg8" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="IP address">IP address</a> 192.168.0.2, an address commonly used within local area networks. He along with others <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Petitions_by_Rio,_et_al,_for_an_investigation_of_the_2022_Philippine_presidential_election_results" id="mwDhA" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Petitions by Rio, et al, for an investigation of the 2022 Philippine presidential election results">petitioned the Supreme Court and later the Comelec</a> to pave the way for an investigation of the phenomenon.<sup about="#mwt1520" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-328"}}' id="cite_ref-328" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-328" id="mwDhE"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDhI"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDhM">[</span>321<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDhQ">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt1523" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"PSJaymalin"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-PSJaymalin-329"}}' id="cite_ref-PSJaymalin_329-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-PSJaymalin-329" id="mwDhU"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDhY"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDhc">[</span>322<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDhg">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt1526" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-330"}}' id="cite_ref-330" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-330" id="mwDhk"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDho"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDhs">[</span>323<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDhw">]</span></span></a></sup> Asked about the allegations in July 2023, COMELEC Chairman <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_Garcia" id="mwDh0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="George Garcia">George Garcia</a> stated that the IP address was accurate, as it was being used within local networks for 20,300 of their modems, and stated that "there was nothing illegal about it. It does not matter if the IP have the same address. What is important is that the transmitted election results are accurate."<sup about="#mwt1527" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"PSJaymalin"}}' id="cite_ref-PSJaymalin_329-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-PSJaymalin-329" id="mwDh4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDh8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDiA">[</span>322<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDiE">]</span></span></a></sup> Election watchdogs, including the National Movement for Free Elections, were present during the testing and aware that only one IP address was used.<sup about="#mwt1528" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"PSJaymalin"}}' id="cite_ref-PSJaymalin_329-2" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-PSJaymalin-329" id="mwDiI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDiM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDiQ">[</span>322<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDiU">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section><section aria-labelledby="International_reactions" data-mw-section-id="49" id="mwDiY"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading3"><h3 id="International_reactions">International reactions</h3><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=49" title="Edit section: International reactions"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p id="mwDic">Marcos was congratulated by diplomats from several countries after initial results showing his victory were released. Chinese ambassador to Manila <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Huang_Xilian" id="mwDig" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Huang Xilian">Huang Xilian</a> paid the president-elect a courtesy visit on May 12, saying China was looking forward “to bring[ing] the two countries' Relationship of Comprehensive Strategic Cooperation to new heights.”.<sup about="#mwt1531" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-331"}}' id="cite_ref-331" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-331" id="mwDik"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDio"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDis">[</span>324<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDiw">]</span></span></a></sup> United States Secretary of State <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Antony_Blinken" id="mwDi0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Antony Blinken">Antony Blinken</a> welcomed Marcos's election, stating that the United States was looking forward to working with him to "strengthen the enduring alliance between the United States and the Philippines."<sup about="#mwt1534" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-332"}}' id="cite_ref-332" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-332" id="mwDi4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDi8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDjA">[</span>325<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDjE">]</span></span></a></sup> Meanwhile, other countries such as <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Japan" id="mwDjI" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Japan">Japan</a> expressed their willingness to work with the incoming administration.<sup about="#mwt1537" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-333"}}' id="cite_ref-333" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-333" id="mwDjM"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDjQ"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDjU">[</span>326<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDjY">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt1540" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-334"}}' id="cite_ref-334" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-334" id="mwDjc"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDjg"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDjk">[</span>327<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDjo">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwDjs">U.S. president <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Joe_Biden" id="mwDjw" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Joe Biden">Joe Biden</a> later called Marcos to congratulate him. Biden said he was looking forward to working with Marcos to strengthen <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philippines–United_States_relations" id="mwDj0" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Philippines–United States relations">Philippines–United States relations</a> and expand cooperation on key issues.<sup about="#mwt1543" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-335"}}' id="cite_ref-335" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-335" id="mwDj4"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDj8"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDkA">[</span>328<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDkE">]</span></span></a></sup><sup about="#mwt1546" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-336"}}' id="cite_ref-336" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-336" id="mwDkI"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDkM"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDkQ">[</span>329<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDkU">]</span></span></a></sup> However, White House coordinator for the Indo-Pacific Kurt Campbell said there were “historical considerations” that could pose challenges.<sup about="#mwt1549" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"auto"},"body":{"id":"mw-reference-text-cite_note-auto-337"}}' id="cite_ref-auto_337-0" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-auto-337" id="mwDkY"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDkc"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDkg">[</span>330<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDkk">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
<p id="mwDko">On May 19, Marcos said that he had held “very substantial” talks with Chinese President <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xi_Jinping" id="mwDks" rel="mw:WikiLink" title="Xi Jinping">Xi Jinping</a>, who had expressed support for his independent foreign policy. He said that ties with China would expand and “shift to a higher gear”, promising to upgrade relations in “not only diplomatic, not only trade, but also in culture, even in education, even in knowledge, even in health, to address whatever minor disagreements that we have right now”.<sup about="#mwt1550" class="mw-ref reference" data-mw='{"name":"ref","attrs":{"name":"auto"}}' id="cite_ref-auto_337-1" rel="dc:references" typeof="mw:Extension/ref"><a href="#cite_note-auto-337" id="mwDkw"><span class="mw-reflink-text" id="mwDk0"><span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDk4">[</span>330<span class="cite-bracket" id="mwDk8">]</span></span></a></sup></p>
</section></section><section aria-labelledby="References" data-mw-section-id="50" id="mwDlA"><div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="References">References</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=2022_Philippine_presidential_election&amp;action=edit&amp;section=50" title="Edit section: References"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>

<li about="#cite_note-:31-5" data-mw-footnote-number="4" id="cite_note-:31-5"><span class="mw-cite-backlink" id="mwDno" rel="mw:referencedBy"><a href="#cite_ref-:31_5-0" id="mwDns"><span names on the 2022 ballot for president, 9 for
